GIS-646 CalDef Database: Version: 7.0.8 Apr 19, 2023 Released by: Andrea Campbell [andrea.campbell@gm.com] Total #CalDefs=3224

CalDef FilenameCalNameUsageDescriptionReferencesCalIDVersionTypeMinMaxResolutionUnitsValue
ADASIS.caldefNAVILINK_ENABLEDMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUEnables/Disables NAVILINK (independent of ADASIS_SUPPORT selection).GIS-628 Appendix B_Yr4q4Ez0Eea8foBPr9M3iw2Boolean     true
ADASIS.caldefADASIS_SUPPORTMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUsee GDMPRS-923, main switch between ADASIS variantsGIS-628 Appendix B_yo_jQE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw5Enumeration     MPP_WITH_STUBS
ADASIS.caldefTx_Max_Offset_RangeMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCU3.4.3 Path Length Limits. The ADASIS concept uses different range values to define the length of the ADAS horizon. GIS-628 Appendix B_yrNw001AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 081901.0m3000
ADASIS.caldefTX_Multiplexed_Minimum_Update_TimeMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUMinimal update interval to send any data on the horizon, shall be filled with retransmission, if no new data is available (see 7.3.4, GDMPRS-802)GIS-628 Appendix B_ysKzIE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Integer 051001.0milliseconds0
ADASIS.caldefTX_Multiplexed_Periodic_RateMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUADASIS transmission update interval for multiplexed outputGIS-628 Appendix B_ysKzIU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Integer 051001.0milliseconds20
ADASIS.caldefTX_Debug_Delay_Initial_TransmissionMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUDevelopment parameter to delay the initial horizon transmission (in milliseconds)GIS-628 Appendix B_ysKzIk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Integer 051001.0milliseconds0
ADASIS.caldefAHP_Tx_Meta_Message_Update_IntervalMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUConfigures transmission of meta message in milliseconds, see table in GDMPRS-923GIS-628 Appendix B_DFSygcRyEeO68uG6rmzOVg4Integer 1000100001.0milliseconds5000
ADASIS.caldefAHP_Tx_Position_Message_Update_IntervalMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUConfigures transmission of vehicle position message in milliseconds, see table in GDMPRS-923GIS-628 Appendix B_W_e5Qb5BEeO1CZP5M-nrYg4Integer 10010001.0milliseconds1000
ADASIS.caldefAHP_Rx_Retransmission_RequestMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUEnables acceptance of retransmission requests from data consuming ECUs, see GDMPRS-997, 998GIS-628 Appendix B_8GNKYcR6EeO1RolFV9aB-w4Boolean     false
ADASIS.caldefAHP_Debug_Enable_VisualizationMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUActivates debugging interface for horizon displays, see. Appendix A, GDMPRS-807,815,816, etc.GIS-628 Appendix B_WVs2EL45EeO1CZP5M-nrYg4Boolean     false
ADASIS.caldefAHP_MPP_RestrictionToFreewayModeMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCU: GDMPRS-999 - The map matching and position refinement function shall exhibit a calibration parameter Ke_FreewayMode. If Ke_FreewayMode=True, the map matching shall assume that the vehicle stays always on roads of class 1 and 2 or the direct connections (exit/entrance ramps) of those roads. GDMPRS-999aGIS-628 Appendix B_73mcEL43EeO1CZP5M-nrYg3Boolean     false
ADASIS.caldefAHP_SecondHorizonParallelInstanceActiveMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUsee GDMPRS-1000. As defined in the GDMPRS-100: The map provider shall be able to send out two instances of an electronic horizon at one time, given the calibration parameter AHP_SecondHorizonParallelInstance=True. GIS-628 Appendix B_7CSIML44EeO1CZP5M-nrYg3Boolean     false
ADASIS.caldefAHP_SecondHorizonOffsetResolutionMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUsee GDMPRS-1000. As defined in the GDMPRS-100: While the first EH shall work with the standard offset resolution of 1m, the second instance shall cover a long horizon of more than 8096m by setting the offset resolution to AHP_SecondHorizonOffsetResolution a higher value such as 10,20,50 or 200m (default:100m). Note: The value range for this calibration is 0-200 meters, and the default value is 100 meters as defined in the GIS-628 Specification.GIS-628 Appendix B_DDJnUL45EeO1CZP5M-nrYg4Integer 02001.0m100
ADASIS.caldefAHP_Content_MinMPPLengthMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUMinimum most probable path length, see Appendix B. The value range for this calibration is 200-800 meters, and the default value is 200 meters as defined in the GIS-628 Specification.GIS-628 Appendix B_SqI_EGYHEea-68x0t4hw1g3Integer 2008001.0m200
ADASIS.caldefAHP_Content_MinSubPathLengthMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUMinimal sub-path length, see Appendix B. Note: The value range for this calibration is 100-800 meters, and the default value is 100 meters as defined in the GIS-628 Specification.GIS-628 Appendix B_001GwGYHEea-68x0t4hw1g2Integer 1008001.0m100
ADASIS.caldefAHP_TrailingHorizonLengthMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUMinimal trailing horizon length, see Appendix B. Note: The value range for this calibration is 0-500 meters, and the default value is 100 meters as defined in the GIS-628 Specification.GIS-628 Appendix B_RcE7EL5BEeO1CZP5M-nrYg4Integer 05001.0m100
ADASIS.caldefAHP_Enable_Extra_Segment_MessageMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUEnable provision of extra segment message as specified in GIS-628 GDMPRS-927. Depending on application requirements and additional SEGMENT message can be provided. Default setting is: false.GIS-628 Appendix B_7ZakcDIaEeaowIzu94EMuA3Boolean     false
ADASIS.caldefAHP_Enable_System_Specific_MessageMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUEnable provision of GM system specific message as specified in GIS-628 GDMPRS-1059. Depending on application requirements and additional GM system specific message can be provided. Default setting is: true, as specified in GIS-628 (Lean ADASIS).GIS-628 Appendix B_YzQMQDIbEeaowIzu94EMuA3Boolean     true
ADASIS.caldefAHP_Enable_Checksum_MessageMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUEnables GM checksum message for configurations in which an ASIL-rated feature is using the data. Values: 0 = false, 1 = true. Depending on application requirements and additional CHECKSUM messages can be provided. Default setting is: false.GIS-628 Appendix B_o8eSkDIbEeaowIzu94EMuA3Boolean     false
ADASIS.caldefAHP_Tx_Enforced_Message_PrioritiesMID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUAllows to enforce horizon data transmission according to message priorities specified by receiving applications (see GIS-628 Sec. 7.1.3). The default calibration for ADASIS message priorities shall be with respect to horizon offset, i.e., FALSE.GIS-628 Appendix B_TZ2k4EeMEeetSriMDOh_Jw1Boolean     false
ADASIS.caldefAHP_Tx_Explicit_STUB_Removal_MessagesMID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUEnables transmission of explicit STUB removal messages (see GIS-628 Sec. 7.3.5). Default: FALSE.GIS-628 Appendix B_SpJgsUeMEeetSriMDOh_Jw1Boolean     false
ADASIS.caldefAHP_MPP_Learning_Mode_EnabledMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUEnables the usage of history and dynamic input for MPP calculation. If Disables, only static map attributes are considered.GIS-628 Appendix B_gWv9cDIcEeaowIzu94EMuA3Boolean     false
ADASIS.caldefAHP_Content_MaxNoSegmentsMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUMaximum number of SEGMENT messages provided by the AHP within the calibrated horizon (GIS-628 GDMPRS-923)GIS-628 Appendix B_PlEQccRzEeO68uG6rmzOVg4Integer 201001.0none90
ADASIS.caldefAHP_Content_MaxNoStubsMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUMaximum Number of Stubs in horizonGIS-628 Appendix B_u_rtwGYGEea-68x0t4hw1g2Integer 0201.0none10
ADASIS.caldefAHP_Content_MaxNo_SP1_CurvatureMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUMaximum no. profiles of that type that shall be part of the horizon, see GIS-628 Appendix B. Adaptation of value range. Default setting for Lean ADASIS is: 0.GIS-628 Appendix B_J00swGYeEea-68x0t4hw1g2Integer 01001.0none0

AOSPVolumeGroupRampTimes.caldefUNMUTE_RAMP_AOSP_VOL_GROUP_RINGMID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALBVolumeGroups organize similar audio streams together under a common volume index. Since users may move through the various VolumeGroups supporting the Mutex Sink, with the possibility of each volume group being at a different step of the index, it is import to ensure that these transitions produce a pleasing audio experience. To facilitate this, the calibrateable ramp times are used to define the mute/unmute ramp rates used when an audio stream is activated or deactivated according to their VolumeGroup. _OJTQ4Y7BEem6S5_uwl3E0g1Enumeration     K_MUTEX_SINK_RAMP_2

AmpInterface.caldefAmplifierTypeLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe CSM/Low Radio shall use the AmplifierType calibration to determine the type of amplifier within the vehicle. When no premium remote amplifier is present, this calibration notifies the CSM/Low Radio of whether a discrete amplifier or remote ANC module are present.GIS-633-3207 and GIS-604-170._skP0gMWPEeOaCvydzCgwdA5Enumeration     InternalAmplifier
AudioHMICals.caldefDEFAULT_DSP_MODEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUDetermines default DSP mode to set based on requirements in UIS-202 and Audio Equalization function modules. Defaults to NORMAL, which is DSP_MODE_3UIS-202 and Audio Equalization requirements_XRjNgMZAEeOsIMxD3Xbm-Q3Enumeration     DSP_MODE_3
AudioHMICals.caldefENABLE_FADELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration enables the appropriate UI display to match the interior vehicle configuration. (FALSE = front row only, TRUE = front and rear) _yqrlVk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw5Boolean     true
AudioHMICals.caldefENABLE_NOISE_COMP_TEXT_STRING_OVERRIDEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUWhen this calibration is set to true, HMI shall show the Vehicle Noise Compensation for the branded audio solution. When this calibration set to false, HMI shall show SCV. _yquBkU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw5Boolean     false
AudioHMICals.caldefNOISE_COMP_TEXT_STRINGMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUWhen ENABLE_NOISE_COMP_TEXT_STRING_OVERRIDE is set to TRUE, the SMT shall set the required text string associated with branded vehicle noise compensation feature. If ENABLE_NOISE_COMP_TEXT_STRING_OVERRIDE set to FALSE, this cal is ignored. Current options: Bose Audio Pilot , Adaptive Volume. This can be changed to other string types using a cal override. _yrT3dU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw5String  31 UTF8Bose AudioPilot
AudioHMICals.caldefSIMULATED_SURROUND_DISPLAY_TEXT_ENABLEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUWhen this calibration is set to true, HMI shall show the surround mode UI for the branded audio solution. When this calibration set to false, the surround mode UI shall be removed. _ysCQMU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Boolean     true
AudioHMICals.caldefSIMULATED_SURROUND_DISPLAY_TEXT_STRINGMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUWhen SIMULATED_SURROUND_DISPLAY_TEXT_ENABLE is set to TRUE, the SMT shall set the required text string associated with branded surround mode feature. If SIMULATED_SURROUND_DISPLAY_TEXT_ENABLE set to FALSE, this cal is ignored. Current options: Bose Centerpoint , Surround. This can be changed to other string types using a cal override. _ysCQMk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4String  11 UTF8Centerpoint
BluetoothFeatureEnables.caldefENABLE_MICROPHONE_2LOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThese Calibrations are desired to be able to enable and disable analog Mic 2 input to CSM. Note: 0 = Microphone 2 is disable, and 1 =ANALOG_DIRECT_CONNECT_TO_CSM for Microphone 2 is enabledGIS-380 & GIS-681_57r_ILsFEeSOH7Idfz1A2w2Boolean     true
BluetoothFeatureEnables.caldefANC_MIC_CONFIGLOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUThese Calibrations are desired to be able to enable and disable input from ANC Mic over EthernetGIS-380 & GIS-681_7YjsILicEeS-LYW3rxjCRA2Enumeration     DISABLED
BluetoothFeatureEnables.caldefMICROPHONE_1_CONFIGLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThese Calibrations are desired to be able to enable and disable analog Mic input from TCPGIS-380 & GIS-681_3mJScLhEEeSbDvnXek4TrA2Enumeration     ANALOG_PASSTHROUGH_DIFFERENTIAL_FROM_TCP
BluetoothFeatureEnables.caldefENABLE_HANDS_FREE_PROFILELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThese Calibrations are desired to be able to enable and disable a complete Bluetooth Profile and its related HMI. _yqrlYE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Boolean     false
BluetoothFeatureEnables.caldefENABLE_ADVANCED_AUDIO_DISTRIBUTION_PROFILELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThese Calibrations are desired to be able to enable and disable a complete Bluetooth Profile and its related HMI. _yqc72k1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Boolean     false
BluetoothFeatureEnables.caldefENABLE_AUDIO_VIDEO_REMOTE_CONTROL_PROFILELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThese Calibrations are desired to be able to enable and disable a complete Bluetooth Profile and its related HMI. _yqqXME1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Boolean     false
BluetoothFeatureEnables.caldefENABLE_PHONE_BOOK_ACCESS_PROFILELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThese Calibrations are desired to be able to enable and disable a complete Bluetooth Profile and its related HMI. _yquBnE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Boolean     false
BluetoothFeatureEnables.caldefENABLE_MESSAGING_ACCESS_PROFILEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThese Calibrations are desired to be able to enable and disable a complete Bluetooth Profile and its related HMI. _yqsze01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Boolean     false
BluetoothFeatureEnables.caldefENABLE_DEVICE_IDENTIFICATION_PROFILELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThese Calibrations are desired to be able to enable and disable a complete Bluetooth Profile and its related HMI _Sv3-kMlgEeOjBciDL3wlpQ2Boolean     false
BluetoothFeatureEnables.caldefENABLE_SERIAL_PORT_PROFILELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThese Calibrations are desired to be able to enable and disable a complete Bluetooth Profile and its related HMI _2r6IEMlgEeOjBciDL3wlpQ2Boolean     false
BluetoothFeatureEnables.caldefENABLE_PERSONAL_AREA_NETWORK_PROFILEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThese Calibrations are desired to be able to enable and disable a complete Bluetooth Profile and its related HMI _Fti7QMlhEeOjBciDL3wlpQ2Boolean     false
BluetoothFeatureEnables.caldefENABLE_BLELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThese Calibrations are desired to be able to enable and disable Bluetooth low energy _rSOaYMldEeOjBciDL3wlpQ2Boolean     false
BluetoothFeatureEnables.caldefENABLE_WBSLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThese Calibrations are desired to be able to enable and disable wide band speech (WBS) _PNMXcMlfEeOjBciDL3wlpQ2Boolean     false
BluetoothFeatureEnables.caldefBT_IN_BAND_RINGINGLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe Bluetooth Module shall be able to turn support for in-band ringing on or off from either the HMI or through a calibration. If the In-Band Ringing calibration parameter is not enabled the Bluetooth Module shall not support in-band ringing by muting the in-band ring tone from the AG (i.e. the Audio Connection) and use an internally generated ring tone in response to the RING alert sent by the AG. _ypggok1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Boolean     false
BluetoothFeatureEnables.caldefK_AUTO_FAN_SPEED_REDUCTION_ENABLEDLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis cal guards the automatic ODI calls from the AVManager to the remote ECC on LSGMLAN via FUClass-7 HVACSettings FanSpeedReduction Action #50. See GIS-302-2997 for detailed triggering requirements. _yrA8lk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Boolean     true
BluetoothHMIPerformance.caldefBT_AUDIO_VOICE_CONNECTION_TYPELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe Bluetooth Module shall be able to support a calibration that utilizes eith eSCO or SCO for the Bluetooth Phone Audio Voice Connection Type. _ypf5kE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Enumeration     ESCO
BluetoothHMIPerformance.caldefBT_DISCOVERABLE_MODE_TIMERLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUIf the discoverable mode timer expires the Bluetooth Module shall notify the HMI and exit the Pairing Process. When a Bluetooth device is not detected within a certain amount of time a popup is displayed. The user may tap retry and the system will go to discoverable mode again with the instructions for pairing displayed. The user may tap cancel or the popup display will time out and return to the application view that Add New Device was initiated from. _ypggoE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 02551.0none180
BluetoothHMIPerformance.caldefENABLE_CALL_TIMERSLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe call info region on the in call view is populated with the active call information. The Call Timer is shown in call information for all types of calls. The Call Timer is shown in HH:MM:SS format. The Call Timer value as provided by the Phone F-Block is displayed. When two calls are active the Call Timer is shown individually for each call and the time update is synchronized. _yqqXO01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Boolean     false
BluetoothHMIPerformance.caldefIGNITION_OFF_IN_CALL_VIEW_DELAYLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUWhen the user turns off ignition with an active bluetooth phone call the display changes to the In Call view as long as there has been no inputs by the user on the current view for TBD time (calibratible). Otherwise the In Call view is delayed until no activity for TBD time (calibratible). _yq7c_E1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 0255001.0none3000
BluetoothHMIPerformance.caldefPHONE_CALL_ENDED_INFORMATION_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUWhen the user initiates ending the current call the current caller hangs up or service connection is lost while in the phone view the phone status will display call ended in addition to the call information for 3 seconds. The user may end the call through the center stack display steering wheel controls or the Bluetooth device. The call timer will stop and flash at a rate of TBD (calibratible). After the time out the ?call ended? indication will no longer be displayed and the caller info region will return to not in a call view. The call timer will no longer be displayed. _yr3RKU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 0255001.0none3000
BrandAnimationsAudioCues.caldefAUDIO_CUE_ACUE_RUN_ENABLEDLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration controls whether or not a Run Audio Cue is played or not. This is reserved only for Electric/Hybrid vehicles. As long as any of the three Audio Cue CALs (Stop, Run, Welcome) are enabled, Audio Cues and Set Audio Cue Volume menus are available. If all three Audio Cue CALs are set to False, then the menu items should be hidden. GIS-500_ypM-oE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw5Boolean     false
BrandAnimationsAudioCues.caldefAUDIO_CUE_ACUE_STOP_ENABLEDLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration controls whether or not a Stop Audio Cue is played or not. This is reserved only for Electric/Hybrid vehicles. As long as any of the three Audio Cue CALs (Stop, Run, Welcome) are enabled, Audio Cues and Set Audio Cue Volume menus are available. If all three Audio Cue CALs are set to False, then the menu items should be hidden. GIS-500_ypM-oU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw5Boolean     false
BrandAnimationsAudioCues.caldefAUDIO_CUE_SHUTDOWN_ENABLELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUCueCalibrations [CueType] These are the boolean calibration flags for each of the cues defined within the AudioCues Fblock. There are currently 11 Cues defined. The CueType takes values from 0x01 - 0x0B. The diagram below defines the processing within the AudioCue FBlock for generating AudioCues relative to vehicle and system state changes All vehicles will have a Welcome Animation Only Hybrid vehicles would have Startup/Run and Shutdown/Goodbye AudioCues enabled. GIS-500_ypM-o01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Boolean     false
BrandAnimationsAudioCues.caldefAUDIO_CUE_STARTUP_ENABLELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUCueCalibrations [CueType] These are the boolean calibration flags for each of the cues defined within the AudioCues Fblock. There are currently 11 Cues defined. The CueType takes values from 0x01 - 0x0B. The diagram below defines the processing within the AudioCue FBlock for generating AudioCues relative to vehicle and system state changes All vehicles will have a Welcome Animation Only Hybrid vehicles would have Startup/Run and Shutdown/Goodbye AudioCues enabled. GIS-500_ypM-pE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Boolean     false
BrandAnimationsAudioCues.caldefAUDIO_CUE_WELCOME_ENABLELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUSee section 5.6.1.2 in GIS. When enabled, an audio cue will play when welcome animation is played. As long as any of the three Audio Cue CALs (Stop, Run, Welcome) are enabled, Audio Cues and Set Audio Cue Volume menus are available. If all three Audio Cue CALs are set to False, then the menu items should be hidden.GIS-500_ypNlsE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw6Boolean     false
BrandAnimationsAudioCues.caldefVEHICLE_ANIMATION_SHUTDOWN_TYPELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUShutdown Animation type The shutdown animation shall be defined by a calibration setting as shown below GIS-500_ysLaM01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Enumeration     FADE_TO_BLACK
BrandAnimationsAudioCues.caldefVEHICLE_ANIMATION_STARTUP_TYPELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUStart-up Audio Cues type The Start-up Audio Cues shall be defined by a calibration setting as shown below GIS-500_ysLaNU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Enumeration     CADILLAC
BrandAnimationsAudioCues.caldefVEHICLE_ANIMATION_WELCOME_TYPELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUAfter powerup the silverbox shall display a Welcome Animation which is specific to the brand of the vehicle. The welcome animation shall be defined by a calibration setting as shown below GIS-500_ysMBME1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Enumeration     CADILLAC
BrandAnimationsAudioCues.caldefVEHICLE_AUDIOCUE_SHUTDOWN_TYPELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUShutdown Audio Cues type The Shutdown Cues shall be defined by a calibration setting as shown below GIS-500_ysMBMk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Enumeration     CADILLAC
BrandAnimationsAudioCues.caldefVEHICLE_AUDIOCUE_STARTUP_TYPELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUStart-up Audio Cues type The Start-up Audio Cues shall be defined by a calibration setting as shown below GIS-500_ysMBNE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Enumeration     CADILLAC
BrandAnimationsAudioCues.caldefVEHICLE_AUDIOCUE_WELCOME_TYPELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUWelcome Audio Cues type The welcome Audio Cues shall be defined by a calibration setting as shown below GIS-500_ysMoQE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Enumeration     CADILLAC
BrandAnimationsAudioCues.caldefWELCOME_ANIMATION_ENABLEDLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration controls whether or not a Welcome Animation is played or not.GIS-500_ysN2gU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw5Boolean     true
CANHandlerCals.caldefBUS_WAKEUP_DELAY_TIMELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALBThe suppliers shall copy the calibration definitions in the GMLCal.c file into their calibration files that have the application defined calibrations. The calibration program editor shall feed these files into the applicable calibration program. The GMLAN Handler is capable of using the new value that was programmed into the flash memory instead of the hard coded default value in the GMLAN Hander. _yphuwE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 02501.0none100
CANHandlerCals.caldefCOMM_ENABLED_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALBThis is the LSGMLAN Vector handler's comm enabled timeout  _yprfw01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 02550.1seconds60
CANHandlerCals.caldefHS_BUS_OFF_DTC_FAILURE_CRITERIA_XMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALBThe handler notifies the application by using a callback function. This callback function is launched by the handler whenever a bus off condition is reported by the CAN-controller. A routine () checks every 1000ms if a Bus Off condition occurred. If the number of Bus Off events exceeds a Calibratable threshold of a calibratable number of consecutive tests i.e. an X of Y algorithm then the Bus Off fault code is set. The X of Y algorithm shall be started and the counter for Bus Off events shall go from 0 to 1 when the application is notified by the handler that a Bus Off has occurred. Setting of Lost Comm DTCs shall be inhibited while the counter for Bus Off events is greater than 0. The HMI Module shall make provisions for these 2 calibration variables. For high speed buses the default value for Bus Off events shall be 5 and the number of consecutive tests shall be set to 5 i.e. x=5 and y=5. _yq5ny01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 02551.0none5
CANHandlerCals.caldefHS_BUS_OFF_DTC_FAILURE_CRITERIA_YMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALBThe handler notifies the application by using a callback function. This callback function is launched by the handler whenever a bus off condition is reported by the CAN-controller. A routine () checks every 1000ms if a Bus Off condition occurred. If the number of Bus Off events exceeds a Calibratable threshold of a calibratable number of consecutive tests i.e. an X of Y algorithm then the Bus Off fault code is set. The X of Y algorithm shall be started and the counter for Bus Off events shall go from 0 to 1 when the application is notified by the handler that a Bus Off has occurred. Setting of Lost Comm DTCs shall be inhibited while the counter for Bus Off events is greater than 0. The HMI Module shall make provisions for these 2 calibration variables. For high speed buses the default value for Bus Off events shall be 5 and the number of consecutive tests shall be set to 5 i.e. x=5 and y=5. _yq5nzE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 02551.0none5

CSM2IPCorTCP.caldefDRIVE_SIDEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALBDRIVE_SIDE, to comprehend vehicle configuration of Right Hand Drive (RHD) versus Left Hand Drive (LHD) with respect to GMLAN message location of driver front versus passenger front. $0- LHD $1-RHD  _ho8dIMZWEeOt4scp4-kueg1Boolean     false
CSM2IPCorTCP.caldefCLUSTER_SWIPE_APPLICATION_EVENT_MAX_NOTIFY_RATEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALBIf the SwipeApplication enumeration value has not changed, but the SwipePositionPercent is changing, the CenterStack HMI shall rate limit notifications of changes in the Property to the internal calibration value CLUSTER_SWIPE_APPLICATION_EVENT_MAX_NOTIFY_RATE. This is necessary to limit MOST control channel loading and will generally be in the tens of milliseconds. This calibration value is defined separately within the calibration specification of the module that drives the center stack HMI. For example, this is GIS-321 for an HMIModule equipped system. _lbsigMl2EeO1nYYt5aEBRA1Integer 021474836471.0milliseconds30

CustomizationGatewayTimer.caldefCUSTOMIZATION_GATEWAY_RESET_TIMERLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUImplemented in MY18 on Global A vehicles as defined in GIS-601-402-1-1 and MY19 on Global B vehicles as defined in GIS-630-3300. FYI: Setting this timer value <= 500 ms may cause timing issues for serial data gateway modules. GIS-601-402-1-1 and GIS-630-3300_i0DswAiXEeW_AJEPDelH5w2Float 0.15.00.1seconds0.5
DirectionalAudioPromptRendering.caldefDirectionalAudioPromptRenderingRangeMap_Custom_3MID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUA DirectionalAudioPromptRenderingRangeMap is an array calibration used within the HMI controller to map the range brackets to a defined zone used for processing Navigation TTS commands which include Center or None as attributes (i.e., not a "right" or "left" turn direction). These zones are identified as Zone 11, 12, and 13. Up to six range brackets can be identified to allow customization for range settings. Distance Bracket = Set to map reported range bracket to a set of audio zones defined by General Motors for this application.  _XXyN0T9EEei2tsrzDiA2EQ1Enumeration     Zone_0
DirectionalAudioPromptRendering.caldefDirectionalAudioPromptRenderingRangeMap_Custom_2MID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUA DirectionalAudioPromptRenderingRangeMap is an array calibration used within the HMI controller to map the range brackets to a defined zone used for processing Navigation TTS commands which include Center or None as attributes (i.e., not a "right" or "left" turn direction). These zones are identified as Zone 11, 12, and 13. Up to six range brackets can be identified to allow customization for range settings. Distance Bracket = Set to map reported range bracket to a set of audio zones defined by General Motors for this application.  _XIEW0T9EEei2tsrzDiA2EQ1Enumeration     Zone_0
DirectionalAudioPromptRendering.caldefDirectionalAudioPromptRenderingRangeMap_Custom_1MID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUA DirectionalAudioPromptRenderingRangeMap is an array calibration used within the HMI controller to map the range brackets to a defined zone used for processing Navigation TTS commands which include Center or None as attributes (i.e., not a "right" or "left" turn direction). These zones are identified as Zone 11, 12, and 13. Up to six range brackets can be identified to allow customization for range settings. DirectionalAudioPromptRendering.caldefDirectionalAudioPromptRendering_EnableMID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUA PromptRendering_Enable calibration shall be provided to serve as a master enable/disable "switch" to allow the feature to be enabled or disabled without requiring a software change. If the feature is turned by setting the PromptRendering_Enable calibration to FALSE, then the HMI display shall not show any UI controls or status information associated with Prompt Rendering. The allows the Prompt Rendering feature to be added/removed using a calibration. _TN76UD_tEeeK3tJi-kcCzA1Boolean     true
DirectionalAudioPromptRendering.caldefDirectionalAudioPromptRenderingRange_EnumMID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUA DirectionalAudioPromptRenderingRange_Enum calibration is used within the HMI controller to define the range bracket enumerations required to support the Directional Audio Prompt Rendering function. _aJmYgD8gEeiX3IvJZ57jLQ1Enumeration     Range_Bracket_0

DirectionalAudioPromptRendering.caldefDirectionalAudioPromptRenderingZoneMapMID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUA DirectionalAudioPromptRenderingZoneMap calibration is defined as an array to map the range brackets and direction to a defined zone. For example, 0, 1, 1 defined in the cal definiton below means: "Range Bracket 0, Direction Left, Zone 6". If the value of zero is selected for this calibration, the CSM shall NOT render this prompt directionally (standard prompt, non-directional). _vXyQ4D9JEeijcMex7f4qKw1EnumIntegerMap 0151.0noneRange_Bracket_0_Direction_0:13

DirectionalAudioPromptRendering.caldefDirectionalAudioPromptRenderingMenuItem_OnMID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUAn HMI calibration defined as Prompt_Rendering_Enable_Menu_Item_On shall be used to set the default (i.e., factory built initial state) for the Prompt Rendering feature in the UI. The default state is ON == Prompt Rendering ENABLED. This allows the feature to be enabled when a User takes delivery of the vehicle. _ZInogD_LEee4MeDnqjMD-Q1Boolean     true
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefQUICKSTATUSPANEWIDGET_AUTOMATICDISMISSALTIMELOW,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUQuick Status Panes auto-dismiss after 3 seconds (calibratable) of inactivity with the vehicle controls. The pane will immediately be removed from the display (no gradual/slow fading). (Note: This calibration is only be used by the F-Ten Info3.0 Low Radio supplier)  _yr6UaE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 02551.0none3
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefPOPUPWIDGET_AUTODISMISSFADEOUTTIMELOW,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUTo offer the user more time to make a final dismiss selection before the pop-up is unavailable the pop-up will fade into the background over a 3 second period (calibratable). UPDATE: ALERT_AUTODISMISSFADEOUTTIME must be in all CALDS bundles and common to all HMI software sets. * However the calibration only functions with 8 systems and shall have default of 3 seconds. * The calibration will perform no action in the 4.2 software sets and shall have default of 0 seconds. auto-dismiss fadeout is not described in GIS-402 and alerts will not FADE out. No transitional animation for 4.2 systems. (Note: This calibration is only be used by the F-Ten Info3.0 Low Radio supplier)  _yr34N01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 02551.0none3
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefD_LONG_AUTO_ACKLOW,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUThe alert will be considered acknowledged when displayed continuously for D_LONG_AUTO_ACK. (Note: This calibration is only be used by the F-Ten Info3.0 Low Radio supplier) _yp4UEk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 0255001.0none25000
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefD_MEDIUM_AUTO_ACKLOW,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUThe alert will be considered acknowledged when displayed continuously for D_MEDIUM_AUTO_ACK. (Note: This calibration is only be used by the F-Ten Info3.0 Low Radio supplier) _yp4UE01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 0255001.0none10000
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefD_SHORT_AUTO_ACKLOW,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUThe alert will be considered acknowledged when displayed continuously for D_SHORT_AUTO_ACK. (Note: This calibration is only be used by the F-Ten Info3.0 Low Radio supplier) _yp4UFE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 0255001.0none2000
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefMINACTIVITYINDICATORENABLETIMELOW,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUThe guideline for displaying an Activity Indicator is when the result of a button press or other system operation will take more than 1 second to complete (e.g. display something relevant to the task being performed) or when some system process is taking place in the background where users should be kept informed (see Applications for specific examples). (Note: This calibration is only be used by the F-Ten Info3.0 Low Radio supplier)  _yrRbRk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 025501.0none1000
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefMINACTIVITYINDICATORACTIVETIMELOW,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUto prevent blings of this indicator it should have a minimum active time. (Note: This calibration is only be used by the F-Ten Info3.0 Low Radio supplier) _yrRbRU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 025501.0none2000
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefALERTWINDOWELEMENT_DELAYEDACTIONPRESENTATIONTIMELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUTime for how long buttons on Alerts and Pop-ups should be delayed before being presented to prevent the user from inadvertently dismissing the pop-up unintentionally. The default time is 500ms.GIS-500_ypCmk01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw5Integer 0127501.0milliseconds500
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefAPPLICATION_REVEALONAPPROACHTIMEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALBThe page indicator is a reveal on approach element. APPLICATION_REVEALONAPPROACHTIME is the amount of time before the reveal starts to fade away.  _ypItMU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 02551.0none15
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefCSHMI_AUX_TOUCH_EVENT_BUFFER_TIMEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALBTo minimize the message load on the MOST network in case the RSE is active on the CenterStackDisplay this parameter gives the time in which touch events are buffered before they will be sent to the RSE-System. The proximity event or the first touch event (start of a gesture) are sent immediately. A release event will trigger the sending of the events buffered so far even if the time for buffering has not ended. _yp4UEE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 02551.0none100
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefCSHMI_TOUCH_EVENT_BUFFER_TIMELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALBTo minimize the message load inside the HMIModule this parameter gives the time in which touch events are buffered before they will be sent to the HMI thread. The proximity event or the first touch event (start of a gesture) are sent immediately. A release event will trigger the sending of the events buffered so far even if the time for buffering has not ended. _yp4UEU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 02551.0none50
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefDEFAULT_AUDIO_TOUCH_FEEDBACK_SETTINGLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUAudible Touch Feedback specifies if sounds play when you press the touchscreen or a physical control on the radio faceplate. _yp_o3E1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Boolean     true
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefENABLE_FACEPLATE_VOLUME_PRESS_HOLDLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUIf the user presses and holds on a side the volume ramps up or down depending on the side of the bar the hold on.GIS-500_yqrlVE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Boolean     true
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefENABLE_FACEPLATE_VOLUME_TAPLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUVolume TapGIS-500_yqrlVU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Boolean     true
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefENABLE_MENU_AUDIO_TOUCH_FEEDBACKLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUMenu Configuration Settings This bit controls the presence of a user selectable menu for audible touchscreen feedback and shall be enabled for all NON-CADILLAC systems with audible beeps and disabled for Cadillac system with Haptic feedback. 2) Potentially need to add new calibration to allow calibration DEFAULTING of the selection of audible touch feedback Currently the MY2014 software defaults the touch feedback setting menu item to OFF.  _yqszek1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Boolean     false
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefENABLE_PROXIMITY_SENSINGMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUMenu Configuration Setting and function enable. Enable for Cadillac. Disalble for all non-Cadillac. Proximity Sensing in the center stack enables specific interaction capabilities with the system. Proximity sensing in the center stack is used to provide feedback to the user and keep the screen clutter-free (i.e. elegant). When a user?s hand approaches the display it comes alive and interactive elements are displayed that the user can touch. Users can interact with the interface in an exciting and delightful way when the system detects their hand approaching the screen. _yquopU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Boolean     false
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefENABLE_REVEALONAPPROACHMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCU9.13.4.4~MY13 Proximity Sensing If the system has Proximity Sensing the Proximity Sensing setting menu should be dynamically added to the Display menu options. The options shown to the user are as follows: ? Off ? On ? On - Map Only The Apps Tray is normally hidden from view. The Apps Tray is revealed on proximity on almost all views. In cases where the Apps Tray is NOT shown it is documented in that section. Apps Tray will fade when reveal on approach time expires.GIS-500_yquoqU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Enumeration     ON
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefFACEPLATE_FLING_MIN_VOLUME_POSITIONSLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis defines the minimum number of volume positions that must be detected before it qualifies the action as a swipe/fling of the faceplate volume.GIS-500_yq2kek1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw6Integer 0631.0none2
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefFACEPLATE_TAP_MAX_TIMELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis defines the amount of time (in milliseconds) that still qualifies the action as a Tap and not a press and hold. Once a press and hold is detected based on this timer, the volume is incremented or decremented by one step, and the timer in VOLUME_PRESS_HOLD_VOLUMESLEWRATE is started to determine when the volume will be incremented or decremented again.GIS-500_yq2ke01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw5Integer 025501.0milliseconds500
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefFACEPLATE_SLIDER_POSITIONS_PER_VOLUME_STEPMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis Calibrations defines how sensitive the volume slider should be to obtain the desired effect for the number of swipes needed to reach max volume. It defines how many slider positions must be traversed before the volume is increased by one step. Higher numbers lead to a less sensitive volume bar.GIS-500_yq2kfU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw5Integer 0631.0none3
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefFACEPLATE_VOLUME_TAP_VOLUME_STEP_DELTALOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis defines the number of Volume Steps each tap on the Faceplate Volume bar either increments or decrementsGIS-500_yq2kfk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 0631.0none1
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefFRONT_FAN_SPEED_ADJUSTMENT_SLEW_RATELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALBFinally users can also increase/decrease fan speed incrementally by tapping at the extremes of the displayed fan speed range (e.g. as denoted by +/- symbols or other icons at the extremes). For each tap fan speed is increased or decreased by one setting value. Users can press-and-hold at the extremes to increase/decrease fan speed rapidly at the rate of 2 Hz (TBD calibratable). _yq4ZqU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 025501.0none200
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefFRONT_TEMPERATURE_ADJUSTMENT_SLEW_RATELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALBFinally users can increase/decrease the ?set? temperature incrementally by tapping at the extremes of the displayed temperature range (e.g. as denoted by +/- symbols at the extremes). For each tap the ?set? temperature value is increased or decreased by one degree. Users can press-and-hold at the extremes to increase/ decrease ?set? temperature rapidly at the rate of 5 Hz (TBD calibratable). _yq4Zqk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 025501.0none200
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefMAX_FACEPLATE_VOLUME_POSITIONSMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration defines the number of slider postitions that are used to communicate the position of the finger from the far left to the far right side of the slider. This is based on the interface between the faceplate and radio.GIS-500_yrNJ1E1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Integer 0631.0none63
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefPOP_UPS_WIDGET_AUTOMATIC_DISMISSAL_TIME_1LOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUPop-ups will auto-dismiss if not explicitly dismissed by the user Pop-ups are triggered to auto-dismiss after 60 seconds (calibratable).GIS-500_yr34Mk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw5Integer 02551.0seconds60
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefPOP_UPS_WIDGET_AUTOMATIC_DISMISSAL_TIME_2LOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUPop-ups will auto-dismiss if not explicitly dismissed by the user Pop-ups are triggered to auto-dismiss after 6 seconds calibratable).GIS-500_yr34M01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw5Integer 02551.0seconds6
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefPOP_UPS_WIDGET_AUTOMATIC_DISMISSAL_TIME_3LOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUQuick Notice long timeout of 5 seconds.GIS-500_yr34NE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw5Integer 02551.0seconds5
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefPOP_UPS_WIDGET_AUTOMATIC_DISMISSAL_TIME_4LOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUQuick Notice short timeout of 3 secondGIS-500_yr34NU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw5Integer 02551.0seconds3
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefREVEALONAPPROACHFADEOUTTIMEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALBThe Apps Tray is normally hidden from view. The Apps Tray is revealed on proximity on almost all views. In cases where the Apps Tray is NOT shown it is documented in that section. Apps Tray will fade when reveal on approach time expires. _yr8JsE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 02551.0none3
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefSCROLLBAR_PAGEUPDOWN_SLEWRATELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALBusers can press and hold on the end-points of the Scroll Bar causing the list to page up/down at a rate of TBD Hz. _ysBCH01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 01281.0none2
DisplayGesturesAndTimeouts.caldefVOLUME_PRESS_HOLD_VOLUMESLEWRATELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis defines how quickly the volume should increase or decrease as the user presses and holds their finger. This calibration defines how many milliseconds should pass in between increasing or decreasing the volume by one step while the finger is held. GIS-500_ysMoTk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw6Integer 025501.0none50
DistortionLimiting.caldefDDL_ENABLELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUAll amplifier outputs including both the CSM and Low Radios internal amplifiers as well as those in a remote amplifier shall provide some form of Dynamic Distortion Limiting to minimize or eliminate clipping of the amplifier speaker output and minimize speaker distortions- The distortion limiting algorithm shall include the following capabilities: 1. The DDL algorithm shall be applied to all amplified speaker outputs. 2. It should be possible, to enable / disable the DDL. GIS-604-240 Dynamic Distortion Limiting (DDL)_eQ3R4DLREeSm0acW-cA1AQ2Boolean     true

DistortionLimiting.caldefFrontRearLimiterCombinationLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUAll amplifier outputs including both the CSM and Low Radios internal amplifiers as well as those in a remote amplifier shall provide some form of Dynamic Distortion Limiting to minimize or eliminate clipping of the amplifier speaker output and minimize speaker distortionsGIS-604-240 Dynamic Distortion Limiting (DDL)_Gfr7sDLREeSm0acW-cA1AQ2Enumeration     FrontRearIndependent
DistortionLimiting.caldefLOUDNESS_OFFSETLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe CSM and Low radio shall implement the LOUDNESS_OFFSET calibration within their ALC process to adjust loudness curves after sound tuning into the optimal position. As an example, in the table in the previous section, the -20dB row represents the frequency response for 82dB(A) sound pressure level on the driver seat, head position. If corrections are needed, the LOUDNESS_OFFSET calibration should be used to move the curve into the right position. The LOUDNESS_OFFSET calibration is defined in the DistortionLimiting.caldef file within GIS-646 with Range of -20dB ... +20dB (1dB Steps) and a default value of 0dB.GIS-604-547_BUk-MDLQEeSm0acW-cA1AQ2Integer -20201.0dB0
DistortionLimiting.caldefLOUDNESS_COMP_ENABLELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUEnables / disables Automatic Loudness Compensation processing. True = Enabled, False = Disabled _87iC8EWqEeSJbrP6o-dHmQ2Boolean     false

DriveModeVisualizationAndConfiguration_GlobalB.caldefTouchZonesHighlight_TimeMID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration controls the timing on the Touch zone highlights. MY 21. Mid / High Radio. Global BGIS-552 5.1.1.1 MY21 Calibration Items_cjd_EE_YEeibCblrVhJh5A1Integer 041.0seconds2
DrivingRestrictions.caldefUNLOCK_DWL_DRIVER_SEATBELT_ATTACHEDLOW,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUMY2014: Modified calibration for Driver Workload to accommodate driver and passenger seatbelt status per FCAT 2.3 Lockouts could be disabled when the following condi­tions are true or have occurred. The system must ac­count for these conditions through calibrations. (Note: This calibration is only be used by the F-Ten Info3.0 Low Radio supplier)  _ysKzKk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Boolean     false
DrivingRestrictions.caldefUNLOCK_DWL_FRONT_PASSENGER_OPERATIONLOW,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCULockouts could be disabled when the following condi­tions are true or have occurred. The system must ac­count for these conditions through calibrations. NOTE: BIT 3 and BIT 2 are mutually exclusive and only one of these bits can be enabled. Both zero is also allowed per design. (Note: This calibration is only be used by the F-Ten Info3.0 Low Radio supplier)  _ysKzK01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Boolean     false
DrivingRestrictions.caldefUNLOCK_DWL_FRONT_SEAT_PASS_AOS_DETECTEDLOW,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCULockouts could be disabled when the following condi­tions are true or have occurred. The system must ac­count for these conditions through calibrations. _ysLaIE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Boolean     false
DrivingRestrictions.caldefUNLOCK_DWL_OPERATION_WITH_WARNING_ACCEPTEDLOW,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCULockouts could be disabled when the following condi­tions are true or have occurred. The system must ac­count for these conditions through calibrations. NOTE: BIT 3 and BIT 2 are mutually exclusive and only one of these bits can be enabled. Both zero is also allowed per design. (Note: This calibration is only be used by the F-Ten Info3.0 Low Radio supplier)  _ysLaIU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Boolean     false
DrivingRestrictions.caldefUNLOCK_DWL_PASS_SEATBELT_ATTACHEDLOW,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUMY2014: Modified calibration for Driver Workload to accommodate driver and passenger seatbelt status per FCAT 2.3 Lockouts could be disabled when the following condi­tions are true or have occurred. The system must ac­count for these conditions through calibrations. (Note: This calibration is only be used by the F-Ten Info3.0 Low Radio supplier)  _ysLaIk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Boolean     false

FrontRearCameraStatic.caldefRVS_OVLAY_TRACK_WIDTHLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCULateral distance between guidelines .GIS 40A_yr_z8U1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Integer 0510020.0mm136
FrontRearCameraStatic.caldefRVS_PRESENT_STATUSLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUCalibration is used to determine if the vehicle has a camera and also determines which type of camera.On/Off Option If the RVS_ FEATURE_AVAIL_VIDEO=2 then the HMI module shall have a Front Camera Display request selection for the driver. This will allow the driver to request the front camera on when the vehicle movement state is equal to forward and the vehicle speed is less than RVS_DISABLE_HYSTERESIS_SPEED (e.g. 7mph). When the front camera view is selected by the driver the HMI module shall send a GMLAN message (Display_Front_360_Camera_Req == 1) to the Video Processing Module to display the front camera view. If the RVS_ FEATURE_AVAIL_VIDEO=2 then the HMI module shall send a GMLAN message (VPM_Video_ICS_Disp_Status == 1) to the Video Processing Module when the VPM video signal is being displayed on the ICS. When the ICS is NOT displaying the VPM video signal then the HMI module shall send a GMLAN message (VPM_Video_ICS_Disp_Status == 0) to the Video Processing Module . NOTE: When RVS_PRESENT_STATUS=2 HMIM software shall ignore rear camera cals - VPM controls camera features. GIS 40A_ysAbAE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Enumeration     PRESENT_GEN1
FrontRearCameraStatic.caldefRVS_STEER_INVERSE_FRONT_RATIOLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUInverse of steering ratio - front wheels .GIS 40A_ysAbAk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 0655001.0E-5none5820

FrontZoneVolume.caldefFront_Zone_Audio_Volume_Control_Menu_Item_OnMID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration is used to set the default HMI menu item setting for the Front Zone Audio Control feature, when enabled.  _VfwvkD5wEeisqKUtCrDaqQ2Boolean     true
FrontZoneVolume.caldefFront_Zone_Audio_Volume_Control_EnabledMID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUThe CSM shall support a calibration defined as FZV_Enabled used as a master enable/disable switch for the feature within the Infotainment system. The default state is ON == FZV ENABLED. When enabled, the feature is active including all UI controls associated with the feature. When disabled, the feature is disabled and all UI is removed from HMI such that none of the FZV UI controls are present. This allows GM to control the feature using end of line calibrations. If the FZV_Enabled calibration is set to TRUE, then front zone volume control functionality, including HMI controls, shall be enabled by the Infotainment system.  If the FZV_Enabled calibration is set to FALSE, then single zone audio (i.e, traditional audio experience) shall be provided by the Infotainment system. If the FZV_Enabled calibration is set to FALSE, then the HMI display shall not show any UI controls or status information associated with FZV feature. _xk2ngD_DEee4MeDnqjMD-Q2Boolean     true
FrontZoneVolume.caldefFront_Zone_Audio_Volume_Control_Sync_Menu_Item_OnMID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUAn HMI calibration defined as FZV_Sync_Control_Menu_Item_On shall be used to set the default (i.e., factory built intial state) for the sync control in the UI. The initial state is OFF == SYNC DISABLED. This allows independent driver and passenger control. _uVlroD_CEee4MeDnqjMD-Q2Boolean     true

GIS1024_TermsAndConditions.caldefEnter_NameMID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUIf the user is driving, the Enter Name field is greyed out and if selected by the user, the Feature Unavailable quick note is shown. The bypass counter is also incremented.GIS524 section 5.5, GIS1000 section 6.2_nlAWUN0kEei01ZUlzIrUtQ1Enumeration     MediumSpeed

GIS309_TouchLINInterface.caldefLVM_TLIN_Node_AvailableMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThe calibration is for the TLIN Master to determine whether to anticipate a response from the LVM node. It is set to True when LVM is a TLIN slave node which publishes the Low Volume Module Message. It is set to False when LVM is not a TLIN slave node and does not publish the Low Volume Module Message.GIS-309-140_chKiYDU5EeWPJoo3krKS0w1Boolean     false

GIS337_ConsumerDeviceProjection.caldefApple_CarPlay_LocationLOW,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUEnable CarPlay location Services. Value is set to be true by default. When this calibration is set to True, it means that location shall be shared with CarPlay. Otherwise when this calibration is set to False, Location shall not be set to CarPlay. (Note: This calibration is currently only needed for the F-Ten Info3 Low Radio supplier) GIS 337-4100, added from CR 1635297_kxu2EMu3EeeTONb8lpYsfA1Boolean     true

GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefContextualWeightFactorMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS 350-207 Holds a non-negative value to weight the task preference of a user for each distinguished task by the typical context it is usually requested from the HMI/Speech system. It weights the final prior probability of a task to be optimally balanced between the context prior probability and the general prior probability: _ySyh4MYiEeO1w62ps1mvqw2Integer 021474836471.0none0
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefAbsoluteDisambiguationThresholdMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-208 Absolute disambiguation threshold: see GIS-350-1205) Constant value Dabs, as threshold for confidence scores in disambiguation lists. A disambiguation list may be rank ordered if the entry with the highest confidence score C1 exceeds Dabs : C1 > Dabs _EVkK0MYjEeO1w62ps1mvqw1Integer 021474836471.0none0
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefDeltaDisambiguationThresholdMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-209 Delta disambiguation threshold: (see GIS-350-1205) Constant value Ddelta, referring to the distance between the two highest confidence scores C1 and C2 in disambiguation lists. A disambiguation list may be ranked ordered if this distance exceeds Dabs: C1 ? C2 > Ddelta. _OMlTQMYjEeO1w62ps1mvqw1Integer 021474836471.0none0
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefUsermodelthresholdMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-246 User model threshold: (see GIS-350-902) real or natural number in the range of the speech systems confidence scores. The threshold allows all tasks with confidence scores below to enter the User Model only under specified conditions to prevent falsely accepted tasks to enter the user Model. _IeIT4Kn6EeSUBfpqmsgk4g1Integer -214748364821474836471.0none6000
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefTCsamplesizeMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-211 TC sample size:(see GIS-350-1303) Natural number S which determines the number of tasks for one sample, while monitoring task completion rate.  _pTl80MYjEeO1w62ps1mvqw1Integer 021474836471.0none12
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefTCmonitorFrequencyMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-212 TC monitor frequency: (see GIS-350-1303) Natural number F which determines the frequency for taking a new sample, while monitoring task completion rate _0HjOoMYjEeO1w62ps1mvqw1Integer 021474836471.0none1
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefTCSNRnormalizerMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-213 TC SNR normalizer: (see GIS-350-1303) Natural number F which determines the frequency for taking a new sample, while monitoring task completion rate _AWMgUMYlEeO1w62ps1mvqw2Integer 021474836471.0none10
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefTCsampleControlFactorMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-214 TC sample control factor: (see GIS-350-1303) Real number s which is a factor to the standard deviation of the sample mean and determines the distance from the mean as lower limit of acceptable task completion behavior per sample. _RYVysMYlEeO1w62ps1mvqw1Float 1.4E-453.4028235E380.1none1.5
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefTCmeansamplesizeMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-215 TC mean sample size: (see GIS-350-1303) Natural number k which determines how many recent samples are included in the current mean of last k samples, while monitoring task completion rate _8fbTAMY4EeOpjZKz3hOPiA1Integer 021474836471.0none3
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefTCmeancontrolfactorMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-216 TC mean control factor: (see GIS-350-1303) Real number m which is a factor to the standard deviation of the overall mean and determines the distance from the overall task completion mean as lower limit of acceptable current mean for the last k samples _48KyMMY5EeOpjZKz3hOPiA1Float 1.4E-453.4028235E380.1none1.0
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefTCoutofcontrollimitMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-217 TC out-of-control limit: (see GIS-350-1303) Natural number K for upper bound of successive samples which are out-of-control regarding task completion. If adaptation is temporarily out-of-control regarding task completion successively for K times adaptation is out-of-control regarding task completion and recommendation value for adaptation to frequency of task is set to 0 _MRwt4MY6EeOpjZKz3hOPiA1Integer 021474836471.0none3

If adaptation is temporarily out-of-control regarding confirmation rate successively for K times adaptation is out-of-control regarding confirmation rate and recommendation value for adaptation to frequency of task is set to 0 _4rtM8MY8EeOpjZKz3hOPiA2Integer 021474836471.0none1023
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefMaximaloutofcontrolnumberMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-232 Maximal out-of-control number: (see GIS-350-1306) If the recommendation value for adaptation is set to 0 for a maximal out-of-control number I, hence adaptation has shut itself of for I times, then the recommendation value is not set back to 1 anymore and adaptation deactivates itself _Dnwq0MY9EeOpjZKz3hOPiA1Integer 021474836471.0none3
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefRDtimeframerecentrejectionsMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-233 RD time frame recent rejections: (see GIS-350-1307) Rejected task data will be monitored by how many rejections occurred in a predefined recent time frame by natural number RD time frame recent rejections, representing the number of days counted from current date _N6WU8MY9EeOpjZKz3hOPiA2Integer 021474836471.0none30
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefDeactivationfrequencyadaptationMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-247 Deactivation frequency adaptation: (see GIS-350-1401) Parameter to manually and externally deactivate and reactivate the adaptation process. Default value is 1 to disable adaptation process. When value is set to 0, adaptation is activated. _4KpiwMZGEeO6mvg1ehG6GQ2Boolean     false
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefRDminimumrejectionsMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-235 RD minimum rejections: (see GIS-350-1307) Natural number representing the minimum number of rejections in the time frame for parameter RD time frame recent rejections, for which reward weight is calculated _erq9EMY9EeOpjZKz3hOPiA1Integer 021474836471.0none2
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefRDminimumconfusionsMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-236 RD minimum confusions: (see GIS-350-1307) Natural number representing the minimum number of confusions between task data k and task data i, for which prior probability for this task data k in dependency to task data i is added as additional prior probability with the default value as specified in GIS-350-904 for unknown task data _khQQYMY9EeOpjZKz3hOPiA1Integer 021474836471.0none3
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefFCtaskdataboundMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-237 FC task data bound: (see GIS-350-1308) real number FC task data bound is the lower bound of most frequently requested task data obtained from the last updated prior probabilities Prior (O) as specified in GIS-350-90. Abnormal frequency changes are controlled for all task data above this lower bound. _Ur-_EA9LEeWwaaRaR87fAw2Float 1.4E-453.4028235E380.1none0.2
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefFCsamplesizeMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-238 FC sample size: (see GIS-350-1308) Natural number S resembling the number of days for each sample, while monitoring abnormal frequency changes. The sample frequency is the same number S, so that the time scale is partitioned by S without time gaps _9SHh8MY9EeOpjZKz3hOPiA1Integer 021474836471.0none2
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefFCmeansamplesizeMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-239 FC mean sample size: (see GIS-350-1308) Natural number k which determines how many recent samples are included in the current mean of last k samples, while monitoring abnormal frequency changes _DpwZcMY-EeOpjZKz3hOPiA1Integer 021474836471.0none10
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefFCsamplecontrolfactorMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-240 FC sample control factor: (see GIS-350-1308) real number f which is a factor to the standard deviation of the sample mean and determines the distance from the mean as upper and lower limit of acceptable frequency changes per sample _K19hcMY-EeOpjZKz3hOPiA1Float 1.4E-453.4028235E380.1none0.5
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefMinimumtaskforpriorMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-241 Minimum task for prior: (see GIS-350-1309) natural number as a threshold for a minimum of observed task data to estimate reliable and representative prior probabilities in the User Model. Below this threshold each prior probability is lowered by a reward weight factor Wp _XliycMY-EeOpjZKz3hOPiA1Integer 021474836471.0none10000
GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefActiontreefactorMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-242 Action tree factor: (see GIS-350-903) natural number n and constant natural number f_act as a weight factor f_act^n in the action tree to normalize the absolute counts of all actions regarding their position in the tree _qZO68MY-EeOpjZKz3hOPiA1Integer 021474836471.0none12

GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefActionnoscoredecreaseMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-245 Action no score decrease: (see GIS-350-1203) list of actions for which original confidence score are not replaced by scores after adaptation if this leads to a decrease in the score. The original score is only replaced if this leads to an increase in the score. The value of this parameter may be all actions _QqeoUMY_EeOpjZKz3hOPiA1Integer 021474836471.0none100

GIS350_SpeechAdaptation.caldefActivationExpertiseAdaptationMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUGIS-350-305 Activation Expertise Adaptation: (see GIS-350-2700) Parameter to manually and externally deactivate and reactivate the adaptation process. GIS500_UI.caldefWCS_WARNING_SCREEN_ENABLELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration enables or disables the Wireless Charging Warning behavior as defined in 6.1.5.3. If enabled the Pop-Up and Quick Notice will be available. If disabled they will not be shown to the customer.GIS-500 8.1.1.1*Calibration Items_SSNSYN94EeaeKuvvdmMijg3Enumeration     Disabled
GIS500_UI.caldefWCS_WARNING_SCREEN_FREQUENCYLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration determines how often the Wireless Charging Warning Pop-up is displayed based on ignition counts. Upon reaching the reminder number, the popup shall be displayed on the next ignition that the customer uses wireless charging. GIS-500 8.1.1.1*Calibration Items_LlLywJlFEea-Ao5FQchwtQ3Integer 02551.0none50

GIS500_UI.caldefLockoutPRNDLSpeedLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe system shall be capable of making specific manual controls, both touch screen and faceplate, inoperable within the user interface (e.g., gray out, ignore faceplate/ SWC switch input, etc.) based on vehicle speed, transmission status, or park brake status. When this calibration is set to $0 Unlock in PARK, the System shall enable dwl lockouts when the shifter is out of PARK gear. If the Calibration is set to $1 Unlock in Drive, then the sysetem shall look at the speed lockout calibration to determine at which speed should be used to enable the DWL lockouts.GIS-500 8.1.1.1*MY17 Calibration Items_bY3tMHDvEeS4SZPm9Z25NA3Boolean     true
GIS500_UI.caldefZoomBubbleDurationMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThe amount of time that the zoom bubble dwells on the display after the user stops interacting with the display/controller that caused the zoom bubble to appear.GIS-500 8.1.1.1*MY17 Calibration Items_Nk6eYG7cEeSssq_awwoZ3g2Integer 05000250.0milliseconds4
GIS500_UI.caldefSpeedLockoutThresholdLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe speed value used for triggering Driver Workload Lockouts. This value is consumed by the Driving Mode support system (see SFS-3 Driving Modes Support) which is being developed by GM.GIS-500 8.1.1.1*MY17 Calibration Items_MOok8KmVEeSBV82hZ9UINQ3Integer 02551.0kmPerHour8

GIS500_UI.caldefDriverWorkloadKeyboardLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUWhen a keyboard is invoked (non-keypad), and the drivingGis500 8.1.2.1*MY17 Driver Workload Restriction Items_EbI0ALiqEeSxl78x5qydAw3Enumeration     MediumSpeed
GIS501_HomeScreenUI.caldefAPPLICATION_HOMESCREEN_PERFORMANCEAPP_ENABLEDMID,HIGH,GLOBALBThis calibration is used to enable the HP1 Performance App on the home screen. This calibration will need to be set to TRUE (ie: enabled) for any vehicle that requires the performance app. The Performance App calibration is used to Enable/Disable the display of this Application on the HomeScreen. When this calibration is set to True(Enabled), this HomeScreen Application shall be displayed on the HomeScreen. When this calibration is set to False(Disabled), this HomeScreen Application shall not be displayed on the HomeScreen.GIS-501_JjMx0BgSEey1B_g7sgX8Bg1Boolean     false
GIS501_HomeScreenUI.caldefAPPLICATION_HOMESCREEN_VEHICLEINFORMATION_ENABLEDMID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUThe Vehicle Information Application provides vehicle status information (e.g. Oil Life, Tire Pressure, etc.). The Vehicle Information calibration is used to Enable/Disable the display of this Application on the HomeScreen and in the App Tray. When this calibration is set to True(Enabled), this HomeScreen Application shall be displayed on the HomeScreen. When this calibration is set to False(Disabled), this HomeScreen Application shall not be displayed on the HomeScreen.GIS501 Section 5.1_e-mgwLtwEei-5bHnoORlAg1Boolean     false
GIS501_HomeScreenUI.caldefAPPLICATION_HOMESCREEN_PA_ENABLEDMID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUThe PA application is an application that gives you the information about PA provided with the vehicle. The PA calibration is used to Enable/Disable the display of this Application on the HomeScreen. When this calibration is set to True (Enabled), this HomeScreen Application shall be displayed on the HomeScreen and in the App Tray. When this calibration is set to False (Disabled), this HomeScreen Application shall not be displayed on the HomeScreen. GIS501 SECTION 5.1_0BkNcdxPEeirwbrltDv1bw1Boolean     false
GIS501_HomeScreenUI.caldefAPPLICATION_HOMESCREEN_APA_ENABLEDMID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUThe APA application is an application that gives you the information about APA provided with the vehicle. The APA calibration is used to Enable/Disable the display of this Application on the HomeScreen and in the App Tray. When this calibration is set to True (Enabled), this HomeScreen Application shall be displayed on the HomeScreen. When this calibration is set to False (Disabled), this HomeScreen Application shall not be displayed on the HomeScreen. GIS501 SECTION 5.1_Uqdz8LtvEei-5bHnoORlAg1Boolean     false
GIS501_HomeScreenUI.caldefAPPLICATION_HOMESCREEN_OWNERSMANUAL_ENABLEDMID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUThe Owner's manual application is an electronic version of the physical Owner's Manual that is typically provided with a vehicle. The Owner's Manual calibration is used to Enable/Disable the display of this Application on the HomeScreen and in the App Tray. When this calibration is set to True (Enabled), this HomeScreen Application shall be displayed on the HomeScreen. When this calibration is set to False(Disabled), this HomeScreen Application shall not be displayed on the HomeScreen. GIS501 SECTION5.1_0iBQ0LtuEei-5bHnoORlAg1Boolean     false

When this calibration is set to False (Disabled), this HomeScreen Application shall not be displayed on the HomeScreen.GIS-501_lQLDcJ6ZEea-Ao5FQchwtQ3Boolean     false

As noted in the GIS-551 Air Quality UI, RPO: "USK" is the applicable RPO for Air Quality Sensor to Determine Availability.GIS-501 (Section#s: 3.7.5 and 5.1.1) and GIS-551 (Section #: 5.1.1)_ACCUMAHfEeiDo-Dq14by9g1Boolean     false

When this calibration is set to False (Disabled), this HomeScreen Application shall not be displayed on the HomeScreen. GIS-501_I5qWUJ6YEea-Ao5FQchwtQ3Boolean     true
