GIS604%20SafetyRelatedVolumeGroupOverrides.caldefSAFETY_VOL_OVRD_DWELLLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUA calibration parameter to hold the volume group override level at for a duration after the activation criteria have ceased.GIS-604-824_5ANYsPO6EeavdZd7UaL_VA3Integer 050001.0milliseconds0
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefHD_Artist_Experience_EnabledMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration is used to enable/disable the HD Artist Experience feature in the CSM/Tuner and center stack controller. When TRUE the CSM/Tuner shall enable the HD Artist Experience feature including the center stack HMI prompt to allow the user to enable or disable the feature during normal use. When FALSE, the CSM/Tuner shall disable this feature, which includes removing any centerstack HMI control to enable/disable the feature. Only the default image will be displayed by HMI controllers when this calibration is set to FALSE. Therefore, the CSM/Tuner shall always command the display of the default image whenever this calibration is set to FALSE OR the user disables the feature through the centerstack HMI control. The default state for this feature shall be TRUE.GIS-605-10000_jzRFUDPuEee2j-RGaKwwEQ1Boolean     true
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefHD_Image_Hold_TimeMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThe tuner shall retain the HD station Primary Art Images associated with the last tuned multicast for the time period defined by the calibration HD_IMAGE_HOLD_TIME. This includes the currently playing song Primary Art Images as well as the subsequent song Primary Art Images, if already received. This provides the ability to re-transmit the previously received Primary Art Images to HMI controllers should the user switch to a new HD station and then tune back to the previous HD station within the HD_IMAGE_HOLD_TIME. However, the calibration value shall be determined between GM, the tuner supplier, and Xperi for overall optimization.GIS-605-10000_0PSBcDGhEeeH7MoU92TqRg1Integer 012001.0seconds300
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefTimeHD_SPSInfoMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis cal defines the time delay before sending SPS information after selecting an unknows SPS. _QcD2EXoeEeSjYPic2Gk-6g1Integer 036001.0seconds5
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefTimeHD_SPSUpdateMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis cal defines the time interval to display SPS info after a loss of the SPS signal. __lwAQXodEeSjYPic2Gk-6g1Integer 036001.0seconds10
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefTimeHDServiceLostMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration represents the time after which a return to MPS shall be done after SPS is removed. This is an optional cal and may not be required for GM programs. Therefore, the default value is set to maximum. _cdaaAHocEeSjYPic2Gk-6g1Integer 036001.0seconds3600
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefHDMetaDataHoldTimeMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUHDMetaDataHoldTime shall provide a time interval in seconds that existing HD SPS metadata remains displayed until replaced with the HD Acquiring Signal message. This calibration is required to avoid excessive toggling of the HD Acquiring Message in intermittent or varying signal reception areas. For example, when the SPS HD signal is interrupted, audio is lost (and output is muted) as the tuner begins a reacquisition sequence. During the reacquisition sequence, the tuner shall not send the HD Acquiring message to HMI until this calibration timer expires. This keeps the HMI display stable showing existing metadata in situations were the signal loss is brief. If the HD SPS signal cannot be reacquired before the value of this calibration expires OR before the HD_DropTimer cal expires (whichever occurs first), then the metadata is removed and the HD Not Available message shall be displayed. Alternately, if the value of this calibration is selected to be less then the HD_DropTimer cal value and it expires before the SPS signal is reacquired, then then the HD Acquiring message shall be displayed and metadata dropped while the tuner attempts to reacquire the SPS signal. If it is not successful and the HD_DropTimer cal expires, then the system shall display the HD Not Available HMI message. If successful (digital lock achieved), the new metadata will be displayed and audio played. The default value of this cal shall be 10 seconds.GIS-605-10000_9Rg2IScCEeSmb8tw1ZVRMw1Integer 5201.0seconds10
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefAM_HDBlendThresholdMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThe AM_HDBlendThreshold is the quality level value at which the tuner begins to blend in the digital AM audio. At a quality (Q) level below this cal value the tuner will begin to fallback to the analog AM broadcast. 1 is defined as the lowest quality 4 the highest. The default value is 3.GIS-605-10000_ypG4Ak1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 141.0none3
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefHD_SwitchingFMBandOnOffSettingMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis is the default value used to enable or disable HD for the FM radio band in the vehicle. The default state shall be TRUE and only overwritten if the user decides to disable HD radio through a centerstack HMI control.GIS-605-10000_eBqz8WEKEeOeJZIDg-p74A2Boolean     true
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefHD_SwitchingAMBandOnOffSettingMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis is the default value used to enable or disable HD for the AM radio band in the vehicle. The default state shall be TRUE and only overwritten if the user decides to disable HD radio through a centerstack HMI control.GIS-605-10000_yqL2EU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Boolean     true
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefFM_AnalogDigitalBlendTimeMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUChange Log for April 16, 2014 ReleaseGIS-605-10000_yq3yk01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 125501.0none1281
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefFM_DigitalAnalogBlendTimeMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis is the digital to analog blend time cal for FM.GIS-605-10000_yq3ym01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 17651.0none765
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefFM_MPSBlendThresholdMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis is the quality level at which the tuner begins to blend in the digital FM audio for HD MPS broadcasts. At a quality (Q) level below this cal value the tuner will begin to fallback to the analog FM broadcast. 1 is lock analog. 2 Favors Analog. 3 Good Balance. 4. Some HD Artifacts may be heard. The default value is 3.GIS-605-10000_yq3ynU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 141.0none3
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefFM_SPSBlendThresholdMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis is the quality level at which the tuner begins to blend in the digital FM audio for HD SPS broadcasts. At a quality level below this cal value the tuner will begin to break digital lock. 1 is defined as the lowest quality 5 the highest. GIS-605-10000_yq3ynk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 141.0none4
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefDisplayAMCategoryMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThe DisplayAMCategory cal defines whether the AM Category menu item (HD Only) should be displayed for HD activated radios. This cal only has relevance for HD activated tuners. TRUE means display AM Category and any associated HD stations. FALSE means do not display the AM Category and associated HD stations. The default value is FALSE. The tuner shall also monitor this cal and provide appropriate action to either support or not support the processing of this feature based on the cal setting. This helps balance and optimize system resources based on required features for a given market. _fIkQIScDEeSmb8tw1ZVRMw1Boolean     false
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefHD_MRCOnOffMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis is a calibration for systems that include HD radio diversity antenna processing. The default state shall be OFF (FALSE) as this is a feature not currently available for Info 3 Programs.GIS-605-10000_TQovwGEJEeOeJZIDg-p74A2Boolean     false
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefHD_DropTimerMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis cal represents a timer that is activated when the tuner loses an HD SPS broadcast due to signal strength or other signal degradation issues. If this cal timer expires, it shall trigger a HD NOT AVAILABLE message on the HMI display. The default value is 6 seconds. This calibration works along with HDMetaDataHoldTime to control the HD information displayed during HD SPS transient and signal loss conditions. Refer to the HDMetaDataHoldTime cal description for additional details.GIS-605-10000_yq5nwk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 0151.0seconds6
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefActivateHDRadioMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUCalibration used to activate HD Radio in the CSM. This calibration enables the hardware and software in the CSM to process HD Radio as an activated feature. TRUE indicates HD Radio is Activated. FALSE indicates HD Radio is Not Activated. The default state of this cal is FALSE (Not Activated). When set to FALSE, the CSM shall not display any HD related menu items.GIS-605-10000_BbTfIf4IEeOVOfEBmrJfSQ1Boolean     false
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefHD_BlendDecisionActiveAnalogToDigitalMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration is used to enable the HD Blend Decision algorithm. The default state is TRUE. When set to TRUE, the CSM shall use the Blend Decision algorithm. FALSE disables the use of Blend Decision.GIS-605-10000_f7mRYGEHEeOeJZIDg-p74A2Boolean     true
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefHD_AMBandBlendDecisionCarrierNoiseMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis is the HD carrier to noise signal level to enable blending for the AM band.GIS-605-10000_xtoLwGELEeOeJZIDg-p74A2Integer 40751.0dB68
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefHD_FMBandBlendDecisionCarrierNoiseMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis is the HD carrier to noise signal level to enable blending for the FM band.GIS-605-10000_HIvpgGEMEeOeJZIDg-p74A2Integer 50751.0dB60
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefHD_FM_MPSBlendRateMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration defines a range for the blend rate for FM MPS broadcasts.GIS-605-10000_gswPQGEMEeOeJZIDg-p74A2Integer 181.0none3
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefHD_FM_SPSBlendRateMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration defines a range for controller the blend rate for FM SPS broadcasts.GIS-605-10000_2G7R0GEMEeOeJZIDg-p74A2Integer 181.0none1
GIS605_HDTuner.caldefHD_AM_BlendRateMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration defines a range for the blend rate for AM broadcasts.GIS-605-10000_AKzKgGENEeOeJZIDg-p74A2Integer 181.0none3
GIS608_LVDS_I2C.caldefVOLUME_ENCODER_TECHNOLOGYMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration indicates the encoder technology used for the volume. For a detailed description see GIS-608-300 ICS Controls I2C Memory Map.GIS-608-300 ICS Controls I2C Memory Map Data Address 0x11_97V7gHxREeaHHOy4fZEGwA2Enumeration     Mechanical_Encoder_0
GIS608_LVDS_I2C.caldefTOUCH_CONTROLLER_CALIBRATION_FILELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis file contains the touch controller calibration and tuning file. This file can be loaded and flashed into the touch controller from the CSM. This calibration defines the specific touch controller IC used in the ICS. This calibration contains the calibration/tuning file for the touch controller. For additional description see GIS-608-100 Calibration Data and Tuning.See GIS-608-100 Calibration Data and Tuning_6jb8IBIEEea05achkF2wgw3BinaryFile     DD080ZA_01A_10AA_V03_20160509.xcfg[56962 bytes]:CRC=3436184834
GIS608_LVDS_I2C.caldefICS_CONTROLS_ON_LVDS_I2CLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUWhen true this calibration indicates that the ICS controls information is on LVDS_I2C. When false the ICS controls information will be on LIN(GA) or CAN5(GB). For additional description see GIS-608-030 I2C Touch Controller & Controls Communication to/from the ICS and the CSM.See GIS-608-030 I2C Touch Controller & Controls Communication to/from the ICS and the CSM_LIopQBIEEea05achkF2wgw4Boolean     true
GIS608_LVDS_I2C.caldefTOUCH_CONTROLLER_ON_LVDS_I2CMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUWhen true this calibration indicates that the touch information is on LVDS_I2C. When false the touch information will be on LIN(GA) or CAN5(GB). For additional description see GIS-608-030 I2C Touch Controller & Controls Communication to/from the ICS and the CSM.See GIS-608-030 I2C Touch Controller & Controls Communication to/from the ICS and the CSM_eLYzYBICEea05achkF2wgw2Boolean     true
GIS608_LVDS_I2C.caldefENABLE_TOUCH_CONTROLLER_CALIBRATION_UPDATESLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUWhen true this calibration indicates the touch controller calibration/tuning file (note: defined by the calibration called TOUCH_CONTROLLER_CALIBRATION_FILE) in the touch controller should be updated. When false the touch controller should not be updated. For additional description see GIS-608-030 I2C Touch Controller & Controls Communication to/from the ICS and the CSM. See GIS-608-030 I2C Touch Controller & Controls Communication to/from the ICS and the CSM_WrIjcBIBEea05achkF2wgw3Boolean     false
GIS609_LicenseComplianceMaterialDisplay_GlobalB.caldefTRANSFER_MESSAGE_RECEPTION_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUMaximum time to wait for a CAN TP Message to start transferring before terminating the LCM File transferGIS-609-2120_mlWDEA5AEea7tNw4LOMC9w3Integer 1601.0seconds30
GIS609_LicenseComplianceMaterialDisplay_GlobalB.caldefTRANSFER_MESSAGE_DURATION_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUMaximum time for a CAN TP Message to finish transferring before terminating the LCM File TransferGIS-609-2120_5cTiAA5AEea7tNw4LOMC9w3Integer 11201.0seconds30
GIS609_LicenseComplianceMaterialDisplay_GlobalB.caldefTOTAL_FILE_TRANSFER_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUMaximum time for a file transfer to occur starting from reception of the first frame of the first CAN TP Message to reception of the last frame in the last CAN TP Message NOTE: Units are MinutesGIS-609-2120_4uojsA5AEea7tNw4LOMC9w3Integer 1201.0none5
GIS611_SystemParameters.caldefHEARTBEAT_PERIODLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUTime interval between each Heartbeat message. GIS-611-1000 / GIS-611-3500_YrdE0LXbEeOM4f5PSFkGdQ3Integer 06553510.0milliseconds200

GIS611_SystemParameters.caldefPROCESSING_TIMELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUTime allowed for a Service to respond to a message. If it will take longer than this, the service shall send a Processing messageGIS-611-5035_jse7QLwbEeOrmODmdn_emg3Integer 06553510.0milliseconds20

GIS611_SystemParameters.caldefCONNECTION_CLOSE_REOPEN_TIMELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUTime a Client has to close and then reopen its end of the TCP connection after Service has issued a fin.GIS-611-4050_iqxxMLwcEeOrmODmdn_emg3Integer 06553510.0milliseconds20

GIS611_SystemParameters.caldefMAX_RETRIESLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUMaximum number of client retries on a Method send. _seJIsLwcEeOrmODmdn_emg3Integer 0655351.0none3
GIS618_SystemState.caldefSHUTDOWN_DELAY_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUTimeout value used to delay transitioning of System State to Sleep to ensure that the external Amplifier completes playing of Shutdown Animation audio cue before CSM/Low Radio/GEM enters Sleep StateGIS-618-537_ofo5EOAtEeeOY4lmtLFarg1Integer 01201.0seconds15
GIS618_SystemState.caldefOFFMODE_ACTIVE_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration shall define the Timeout value used to force an exit from HMIInactive.Inactive Substate to Sleep State to ensure that the CSM/Low Radio does not remain awake when an event to trigger the transition to the OffModeActive Substate does not occur.GIS-618-525_pExQQJ8PEeeKqKPvPjizjg2Integer 01201.0seconds30
GIS618_SystemState.caldefREMOTE_REFLASH_PROGRAMMING_COMPLETE_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration shall define the Timeout value used to force an exit from RemoteReflashProgramming.RemoteReflashProgrammingComplete substateGIS-618-530_lt0YAJ8OEeeKqKPvPjizjg2Integer 01201.0seconds30
GIS618_SystemState.caldefPOWERMODE_FAILURE_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration shall define the Timeout value used to force Infotainment Power Mode to change from LAST_KNOWN Vehicle Power Mode to Off in the event of Power Mode Master and Backup Power Mode Master failure.GIS-618-535_Es-7sJ8LEeeKqKPvPjizjg2Integer 01201.0seconds60
GIS618_SystemState.caldefINFOTAINMENT_ACTIVITY_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration shall define the timeout value used to assure that upon timer expiration there is no Infotainment activity for the CSM to support and the Infotainment System is now ready to shut down.GIS-618-536_GlteEJ7uEeeKqKPvPjizjg2Integer 01201.0seconds30
GIS618_SystemState.caldefSUSPEND_PREPARATION_TIMEOUTMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration shall define the Timeout value used to monitor Suspend-To-RAM preparation. Upon Timeout VIP will send SUSPEND_P2 request to SOC to start next level of suspension.PLC_jwMdUKL2EeeKqKPvPjizjg1Integer 10601.0seconds20
GIS618_SystemState.caldefSUSPEND_TIMEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration shall define the Timeout value used to monitor Suspend-To-RAM execution. Upon Timeout VIP will continue Suspend operation ignoring the SuspensionCompletion ACK from the SOC.TBD_frBNIKL1EeeKqKPvPjizjg1Integer 3101.0seconds3
GIS618_SystemState.caldefRESUME_FAULT_TIMEOUTMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration shall define the Timeout value used to monitor Resume status. Started upon starting to resume SOC, and stop upon receiving power level 3 message as Resumption Success ACK. Upon timeout VIP will reset the whole system as SOC failed to resume. It is implemented by the internal PowerLifeCycle state machine.PLC_rukMoKL3EeeKqKPvPjizjg1Integer 10601.0seconds10
GIS618_SystemState.caldefMAXIMUM_FLUSH_FAULT_TIMEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration shall define the maximum length of time for a flush cycle to complete before a failsoft full shutdown is performed. It is implemented by the internal PowerLifeCycle state machine.GIS-618-520_ivZ-kJ7sEeeKqKPvPjizjg1Integer 10800972001.0seconds36000
GIS618_SystemState.caldefRSI_ANIMATION_IGNORELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUUsed in SystemState Logic state charts to include/exclude the RSI for coordination of visual animation playGIS-618-492_ftQZgJ1REeaPtZlN3j40_w2Boolean     true
GIS618_SystemState.caldefCLUSTER_ANIMATION_IGNORELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUUsed in SystemState Logic state charts to include/exclude the Instrument Panel Cluster for coordination of visual animation playGIS-618-494_JIsyAJ1SEeaPtZlN3j40_w2Boolean     false
GIS618_SystemState.caldefRSI_HMIREADY_IGNORELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUUsed in SystemState Logic state charts to include/exclude the RSI for coordination of HMI initializationGIS-618-496_q75mwJ1SEeaPtZlN3j40_w2Boolean     true
GIS618_SystemState.caldefCLUSTER_HMIREADY_IGNORELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUUsed in SystemState Logic state charts to include/exclude the Instrument Panel Cluster for coordination of HMI initializationGIS-618-498_EGKPwJ1TEeaPtZlN3j40_w2Boolean     false
GIS618_SystemState.caldefDISABLED_STATE_OFFMODE_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUTimeout value used to force an exit from Disabled State (TheftLock, NoVIN, NoCAL) to Sleep State when the Disabled State is entered while System Power Mode is Off.GIS-618-490_rSx-kDftEeauU9tExYHaqA2Integer 01201.0seconds30
GIS618_SystemState.caldefUPDATE_AVAILABLE_LOCAL_INFOTAINMENT_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUTimeout value used to force an exit from UpdateAvailable LocalInfotainment whan a remote reflash update is available and the customer does not decide to accept, dismiss or delay the install / download of the update.GIS-618-485_5HqEgHGjEeSgusjZWIuo6A3Integer 01201.0seconds60
GIS618_SystemState.caldefMASTER_OFFMODE_ACTIVE_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUTimeout value (in minutes) used to force an exit from HMIInactive to Sleep during an off mode active event for fail safe in case OffModeActiveRequest = FALSE was not received.GIS-618-480_JHmvgHGjEeSgusjZWIuo6A2Integer 04801.0none35
GIS618_SystemState.caldefCRITICAL_STATE_OF_CHARGE_IGNORELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUUsed within state charts in 618 to bypass BatteryStateOfChargeCriticallyLow indication and behavior.GIS-618-410_ghWF4LmzEeONt_iAU7Ji9A2Boolean     true
GIS618_SystemState.caldefLOCAL_INFOTAINMENT_CANCEL_MODELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUUsed to define trigger condition for exiting LocalInfotainment.GIS-618-420_sruYELmzEeONt_iAU7Ji9A2Enumeration     DRIVER_DOOR
GIS618_SystemState.caldefBACKGROUND_SLEEP_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUSystemState component sleep background timeoutGIS-618-430_3nVKALmzEeONt_iAU7Ji9A2Integer 018001.0seconds10
GIS618_SystemState.caldefSLEEP_FAULT_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUTimeout value used to prevent a battery drain in the event of a software fault that prevents the system from going to Sleep. Also used to limit maximum time in LocalPhone state.GIS-618-440_IXWKkLm0EeONt_iAU7Ji9A2Integer 072001.0seconds900
GIS618_SystemState.caldefVEH_INITIATED_LOCAL_INFOTAINMENT_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUTimeout value that automatically terminates VehicleInitiated LocalInfotainment if the user fails to open the door or remove the key for automatic exit. GIS-618-450_ffiOILm0EeONt_iAU7Ji9A2Integer 072001.0seconds600
GIS618_SystemState.caldefUSER_INITIATED_LOCAL_INFOTAINMENT_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUTimeout value for termination of UserInitiated LocalInfotainment.GIS-618-460_w4eP4Lm0EeONt_iAU7Ji9A2Integer 072001.0seconds600
GIS618_SystemState.caldefSTARTUP_DELAY_WAIT_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUTimeout value used to trigger the startup animation after evPowerModeOn in AnimationInit and HMIInactive states.GIS-618-465_C0yrgLm1EeONt_iAU7Ji9A2Integer 020001.0milliseconds200
GIS618_SystemState.caldefSTARTUP_FAULT_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUTimeout value used to force an exit in the event of a run-reset in either the CenterStackHMI or the ClusterHMI during the StartupAnimation that would hold the SystemState here indefinitely.GIS-618-470_RgQaYLm1EeONt_iAU7Ji9A2Integer 02551.0seconds15
GIS618_SystemState.caldefLOCAL_VALET_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUTimeout value used to limit time allowed for customer to enter the PIN to unlock and gain access to the vehicle storage compartment.GIS-618-475_fscMQLm1EeONt_iAU7Ji9A2Integer 018001.0seconds30
GIS618_SystemState.caldefVIN_RELEARN_ENLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUAs part of the Component-Level TheftLock and VIN Learning Logic, the internal ModuleTheftLockedFlag within a component may only be cleared upon receipt of the evClearVINCPID event. This event is generated in response to the VIN clearing CPID $FE that is sent to the CSM/Low Radio from a service tool. Note that clearing back to a NoVIN condition is guarded by the CSM/Low Radios Internal Manufacturing Enable Counter or MEC as well as a VIN_RELEARN_EN calibration that is used to bypass VIN learning altogether in particular regions where Theftlock is not required but the same end model part number is used for a component. GIS-618-1810_AUJ78LnPEeONt_iAU7Ji9A3Boolean     true
GIS618_SystemState.caldefSUSPEND_TO_RAM_ENABLEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUMaster switch to enable or disable Suspend to RAM functionality. Default value must always be False to reduce probability of mis-calibration.This calibration shall allow the CSM to enable or disable Suspend-To-RAM functionality. When this calibration is enabled (i.e. set to a value equal to TRUE), the CSM shall enable the Suspend-To-RAM feature. When this calibration is disabled (i.e. set to a value equal to FALSE), the CSM shall disable the Suspend-To-RAM feature.GIS-618-510_koSLcDb7EeanKtszDwv8Ww2Boolean     true
GIS618_SystemState.caldefMAXIMUM_SUSPEND_TIMEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUMaximum Suspend time for Suspend To RAM feature. Maximum has been set to 68 hours which simplifies implementation of timer within CSM VIP processor architecture. This calibration shall define the maximum length of time in suspended mode before a full shutdown must be performed in order to preserve vehicle battery capacity. Upon timeout VIP will resume SOC to start Flush-from-Suspend procedure.GIS-618-515_yTW0UDb7EeanKtszDwv8Ww2Integer 02448001.0seconds86400
GIS618_SystemState.caldefMAXIMUM_RESUME_TIMEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUMaximum allowed time for Resume from Suspended state before the SSTL logic takes failsoft action and resets the main SOC.GIS-618-520_mD70oDb8EeanKtszDwv8Ww1Integer 040001.0milliseconds500
GIS618_SystemState.caldefFLUSH_LIMIT_TIMEMID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALBThis is the maximum cumulative time in 'Hours' during which the ECU remains in PowerMode On state, across ignition cycles, between periods for which it is suspended, before a Shutdown-with-Reset request is sent to the PLC State Controller.FG.04.03-2.2.26_72kLwIbfEeuBFcojSjsoPw15Integer 0481.0hours24
GIS618_SystemState.caldefSTR_MIN_TEMP_THRESHOLDMID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALBMinimum Battery Temperature Threshold value used to determine the IsBatteryConditionGood Function outcome.FG.04.03_2.2.29_BO_-IIbiEeuBFcojSjsoPw15Integer -1001001.0degrees-10
GIS618_SystemState.caldefSTR_MODE_SOC_MIN_THRESHOLD_1MID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALBMinimum Battery Charge Threshold 1 value used in MAXSUSPEND TIMER VALUE CALCULATIONFG.04.03_2.2.30_kPnoQIbiEeuBFcojSjsoPw15Integer 01001.0percent90
GIS618_SystemState.caldefSTR_MODE_SOC_MIN_THRESHOLD_2MID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALBMinimum Battery Charge Threshold 2 value used in MAXSUSPEND TIMER VALUE CALCULATIONFG.04.03_2.2.31_L6y9AIbjEeuBFcojSjsoPw15Integer 01001.0percent75
GIS618_SystemState.caldefSTR_MODE_SOC_MIN_THRESHOLD_3MID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALBMinimum Battery Charge Threshold 3 value used in MAXSUSPEND TIMER VALUE CALCULATIONFG.04.03_2.2.32_TyKFYIbkEeuBFcojSjsoPw15Integer 01001.0percent60
GIS618_SystemState.caldefMAX_SUSPEND_TIME_1MID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALBMax Suspend Time 1 corresponding to the Battery Charge Threshold 1 used in MAXSUSPEND TIMER VALUE CALCULATIONFG.04.03_2.2.33_jes2wIbkEeuBFcojSjsoPw15Integer 01201.0hours84

GIS619_BrightnessControl_GlobalA.caldefLAG_FILTER_LOW_END_THRESHOLDLOW,MID,HIGH,GLOBALA,ENTRY_VCU,VCULag filter low end threshold. GIS619_BrightnessControl_GlobalA.caldefINDICATOR_BRIGHTNESS_SOURCELOW,MID,HIGH,GLOBALA,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis enumeration determines which CAN signal shall be used to compute the indicator brightness. GIS621_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDTC_MASK_B395A_72MID,HIGH,GLOBALA,VCUDTC_MASK_B395A_72 - Alive Counter Incorrect/Not Updated  GIS-621 Section 22.3.17_QWwcoEFnEeaX676o0O3GfQ2Boolean     true
GIS621_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDTC_MASK_B395A_3AMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,VCUB395A_3A - Wrong Camera Installed GIS-621 Section 22.3.17_qyZDEePrEeayQ7dTF1k6LA1Boolean     true
GIS621_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDTC_MASK_B395A_08MID,HIGH,GLOBALA,VCUB395A_08  Invalid/Incorrect/loss of Low Voltage Differential Signal Sync Pulse GIS-621 Section 22.3.17_6SSVUEFlEeaX676o0O3GfQ2Boolean     true
GIS621_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDEBOUNCE_TIMEMID,HIGH,GLOBALADebounce Time shall be 2.5 Times the Timeout (timeout is 2.5 times the nominal periodic rate of the signal.GIS-621 1.12.1_zPjjcBObEeWRRp_APrKM0Q2Integer 0100001.0milliseconds2500
GIS621_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDTC_CRANK_DELAY_TIMEMID,HIGH,GLOBALADTC Enable Criteria. It is important that the test enable criteria exclude power modes where the CSM MODULE is not supposed to be fully functional. If a DTC is enabled in Crank Request this has to be motivated and clearly shown that no false (ghost) codes will be set. For DTCs enabled in Off and Accessory power mode, they shall be linked with a secondary parameter (i.e. system voltage range) that clearly inhibit setting false (ghost) codes in under-voltage conditionsGIS-621 -> 13.11_iBYC0JPjEeObkJNbevKUAw1Integer 02551.0seconds5
GIS621_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefVN_ACTIVATIONMID,HIGH,GLOBALADTC_ENABLE_CRITERIA -Each fault detection algorithm may only be active (test running) in specific vehicle power modes or when a specific Virtual Network is active. All power modes/VN active states where the fault detection algorithm is active shall be specified for each DTC.GIS-621 1.12.1_Btq70JjOEeOY6q-k73fxZA1Boolean     true
GIS621_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefRECOVERY_UNDER_OVER_VOLTAGEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,VCUDTC_ENABLE_CRITERIA - All DTC's should not set within 5 seconds of a under or over voltage condition.GIS-621 1.12.1_Cww7EJjPEeOY6q-k73fxZA2Integer 9161.0none9
GIS621_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDTC_IGN_CYCLE_CTR_MAXMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,VCUThere shall be an automatic clearing of DTC Information method implemented within the CSM MODULE. All DTC Status and Failure Type information shall be cleared within the CSM Module when the Fault Free Ignition Cycle Counter reaches [calibration: DTC_IGN_CYCLE_CTR_MAX]. There shall be a single Fault Free Ignition Cycle Counter in the ECU which tracks the automatic clearing of DTC Information for all DTCs supported within the CSM MODULE (i.e., the report of a test fail result for any DTC supported by the ECU will reset the Fault Free Ignition Cycle Counter.)GIS-621 4.1.5_sCm2wJwJEeSUfZTikgbI3Q2Integer 0401.0none0
GIS621_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDID_MASK_2FMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,VCUDID $2F  VEHICLEIDENTIFICATIONNUMBER DIGITS 2-17. This DID contains the Vehicle Identification Number. The data content shall be specified by the vehicle manufacturer. If the VIN has not yet been programmed into the ECU's permanent memory, those locations shall be either '$00' or '$FF'.GIS-621 4.1.7_In5RoJwJEeSUfZTikgbI3Q1Boolean     false
GIS621_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDID_B6_BYTE_1MID,HIGH,GLOBALA,VCUDID_B6_BYTE_1_MODEL_YEAR - This byte is an USN value that represents the target model year using the year 1900 as the base year. To represent the 2000 model year, the value of byte 1 would be 100 or ($64). This is derived from 1900 + 100 = 2000. If the module supports multiple model years, then this byte is filled in with $23 (ASCII equivalent to #).GMW 3110 Appendice C_6dugwLXAEeOAHtc5B2bVlA1Integer -214748364821474836471.0none0
GIS621_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDID_B6_BYTE_2MID,HIGH,GLOBALA,VCUDID_B6_BYTE_2_VEHICLE_MAKE - This byte is the ASCII representation of the Vehicle Make as Identified in the Vehicle Identification Number (VIN). For example, Cadillac = 6 or $36. If the part is used in multiple vehicle makes, then a value of $23 (ASCII #) is used. GMW 3110 Appendice C_CP1LILXBEeOAHtc5B2bVlA1String  100 UTF8TODO
GIS621_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDID_B6_BYTE_3MID,HIGH,GLOBALA,VCUDID_B6_BYTE_3_VEHICLE_CAR_LINE - This byte is an ASCII character representing the vehicle car line (e.g., J for Chevy Cavalier). If the module can be used in multiple car lines, then a value of $23 (ASCII ???#???) is used.GMW 3110 Appendice C_LW1y8LXBEeOAHtc5B2bVlA1String  100 UTF8TODO
GIS621_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDID_B6_BYTE_4_5MID,HIGH,GLOBALA,VCUDID_B6_BYTE_4_5_ENGINE_RPO - Bytes 4 and 5 contain the ASCII representation of the last two characters of the engine Regular Production Option (RPO). The first character of an engine RPO is constant and is therefore not stored. For the L67 engine, the value for these two bytes would be 6 = $36, and 7 = $37. If the module is to be used with multiple engines, then a $23 (ASCII #) is put in both bytes. If the module can be used with any engine type, then $2A (ASCII *) is put in both bytes. GMW 3110 Appendice C_TCl9sLXBEeOAHtc5B2bVlA1String  100 UTF8TODO

GIS621_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDTC_MASK_B101D_3CMID,HIGH,GLOBALAB101D_3C_ELECTRONIC CONTROL UNIT HARDWARE. Vehicle configuration not programmed - This sub type is used to indicate the need to enter (program) the vehicle option content.GIS-621 Section 22.2.2_uxCk8JKGEeOH48DtJo_c7Q1Boolean     true

GIS621_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDTC_MASK_U0020_00MID,HIGH,GLOBALAU0020_00_LOW SPEED_CAN COMMUNICATION_BUS_PERFORMANCE - Upon handler notification that Source Id 0xFF has failed. Source Id 0xFF is used for all signals that have not yet been associated to a source Id. GIS-621 Section 22.4.1_kMtBkLbQEeSqYowDGAuOkA3Boolean     true
GIS621_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDTC_MASK_U0073_00MID,HIGH,GLOBALAU0073_00_HIGH_SPEED_CAN_BUS_OFF - The handler notifies the application by using a callback function. This callback function is launched by the handler whenever a bus off condition is reported by the CAN-controller. A routine () checks every 1000ms if a Bus Off condition occurred. If the number of Bus Off events exceeds a Calibratable threshold of a calibratable number of consecutive tests, i.e. an X of Y algorithm, then the Bus Off fault code is set. The X of Y algorithm shall be started and the counter for Bus Off events shall go from 0 to 1 when the application is notified by the handler that a Bus Off has occurred. Setting of Lost Comm DTCs shall be inhibited while the counter for Bus Off events is greater than 0. The CSM Module shall make provisions for these 2 calibration variables. For high speed buses the default value for Bus Off events shall be 5, and the number of consecutive tests shall be set to 5, i.e. x=5 and y=5.GIS-621 Section 22.4.2_F_6CYJVzEeO7UP4sq3M7sQ1Boolean     true
GIS621_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDTC_MASK_U0078_00MID,HIGH,GLOBALAU0078_00_CONTROL_MODULE_LOW_SPEED_COMMUNIATIONGIS-621 Section 22.4.3_EgXiQJVzEeO7UP4sq3M7sQ1Boolean     true

GIS625_SXMTuner.caldefSXM_Default_PresetsLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe calibration value is defined as a SID. (e.g. SID=13 is SXM ch 2 for Hits 1.) SIDS may change so this is as an example. There are a total of 10 spots (array 0-9) for default presets defined in a single array. Not all spots are necessary to be filled based on program decision. For example, if 4 spots are needed, then 4 values in the array (0 to 3) can be defined to represent each favorite. The remaining values (4 thru 9) are defined as SID=0. If the SID value is 0 (Zero), no preset definition is required. The last preset on a first page needs to be kept for " Hold to Set" Therefore if there are 5 presets spots on the page, only 4 needs to be defined in cal and the last would remain as " Hold to Set". See appropriate GIS specs. e.g. GIS-625, 757, 502, etc.GIS-625-10000_N6PzkDWvEeehCq1AckY2Kg1IntegerArray 09991.0none[0]=13
[1]=46
[2]=16
[3]=132
[4]=0
[5]=0
[6]=0
[7]=0
[8]=0
[9]=0
GIS625_SXMTuner.caldefExplicitContentFilterActiveLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration is used to enable/disable the SXM tuner processing explicit channels. When set to TRUE, the SXM tuner shall filter out explicit channels. When set to FALSE, the SXM tuner shall not filter explicit channels. The default state is FALSE. _SCmxYScBEeSmb8tw1ZVRMw3Boolean     false
GIS625_SXMTuner.caldefInstantReplyActiveLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration is used to enable/disable the SXM Instant Replay feature. When set to TRUE, the SXM Instant Replay feature shall be enabled. When set to FALSE, the SXM Instant Replay feature shall be disabled. _q_IyQd7eEeSfuIbaAz9ajg3Boolean     false
GIS625_SXMTuner.caldefSXM_ArtistSongListViewEnabledLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe SXM_ArtistSongListViewEnabled calibration is used to enable/disable the Artist and Song metadata from appearing in the combined Channel List view. The combined Channel List view includes Channel Number, Channel Name, Artist Name, and Song Name. The default state for this calibration is TRUE. _yq0IME1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Boolean     true
GIS625_SXMTuner.caldefSXM_EnabledLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUSXM Enable cal. Used to turn off SXM when not regionally supported.  _ysOddU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Boolean     false
GIS625_SXMTuner.caldefSXM_FastSeekRateLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe SXMFastSeekRate calibration is used to define the rate the tuner will traverse through the available SXM channels during a user Press/Hold event. The default value is 10 channels/second. _JLZyYTRCEeS02-rXT2e8SA3Integer 5201.0none10
GIS625_SXMTuner.caldefSportsFlashActiveLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration is used to enable/disable the SXM Sports Flash feature in the tuner and the HMI display information associated with this feature. When set to TRUE, the tuner shall enable Sports Flash and associated HMI display item(s). When set to FALSE, the tuner shall disable Sports Flash and the associated HMI display item(s). The default state is TRUE. _uhxykd7eEeSfuIbaAz9ajg3Boolean     false
GIS625_SXMTuner.caldefTuneMixActiveLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration is used to enable/disable the SXM Tune Mix feature. When set to TRUE, the SXM Tune Mix feature shall be enabled. When set to FALSE, the SXM Tune Mix feature shall be disabled. _jtctUN7eEeSfuIbaAz9ajg3Boolean     false
GIS625_SXMTuner.caldefTuneStartActiveLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration is used to enable/disable the SXM Tune Start feature. When set to TRUE, the SXM Tune Start feature shall be enabled. When set to FALSE, the SXM Tune Start feature shall be disabled. _oXI1Qd7eEeSfuIbaAz9ajg3Boolean     false

GIS641_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalB.caldefDTC_MASK_U0155_00MID,HIGH,GLOBALBLOST COMMUNICATION WITH INSTRUMENT PANEL CLUSTER. Upon notification by the handler that the associated supervised signal has failed supervision (typically 2.5 times the nominal periodic rate of the monitored signal). GIS641_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalB.caldefDTC_MASK_U1962_00MID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCUUnable to Authenticate Serial Data Message - $00: No Sub Type Information. Whenever a Message Authentication Code (MAC) results in failed verification, an error counter is incremented for the specific key slot used to verify the MAC except when the conditions to set U1961 92 are present. When the conditions to set U1961 92 are present, the MAC verification failure was caused by Security Peripheral hardware specific errors and not issues with key provisioning.GIS-641_xLQcgBtfEeaVv4YxjvxKoQ2Boolean     true

GIS641_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalB.caldefDTC_MASK_B19B1_50MID,HIGH,GLOBALB,VCURadio System Programming Failure. GIS643_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDTC_MASK_U0020_00LOW,GLOBALA,ENTRY_VCUU0020_00_LOW SPEED_CAN COMMUNICATION_BUS_PERFORMANCE - Upon handler notification that Source Id 0xFF has failed. Source Id 0xFF is used for all signals that have not yet been associated to a source Id.GIS-643_TOgokO41EeeJ-4EGVvY9Kg1Boolean     true
GIS643_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDEBOUNCE_TIMELOW,GLOBALA,ENTRY_VCUDTC_ENABLE_CRITERIA - The absence of required nodes shall be mapped to a Lost Communication with {physical ECU} Trouble Code. There shall be an additional adjustable debounce time for each DTC, which shall be 2.5 Times the Timout. If a time-out occurs, the corresponding U-code must be stored in the state current and failure debouncing shall begin. If required, back-up values might be used for missing signals. Within the error debounce time the supervising ECU must heal current failures if the corresponding signal appears again. If healing is not successful within the debounce time, the failure transition to the state history. _HKpgcN5hEeW58O1OdVks4w1Integer 0100001.0milliseconds2500
GIS643_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDTC_CRANK_DELAY_TIMELOW,GLOBALA,ENTRY_VCUDTC Enable Criteria. It is important that the test enable criteria exclude power modes where the Low radio MODULE is not supposed to be fully functional. If a DTC is enabled in Crank Request this has to be motivated and clearly shown that no false (ghost) codes will be set. For DTCs enabled in Off and Accessory power mode, they shall be linked with a secondary parameter (i.e. system voltage range) that clearly inhibit setting false (ghost) codes in under-voltage conditions _NvWtEEfTEeW-6Omy13RgGA2Integer 02551.0seconds5
GIS643_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefVN_ACTIVATIONLOW,GLOBALA,ENTRY_VCUDTC_ENABLE_CRITERIA -Each fault detection algorithm may only be active (test running) in specific vehicle power modes or when a specific Virtual Network is active. All power modes/VN active states where the fault detection algorithm is active shall be specified for each DTC. _1BEY8EfTEeW-6Omy13RgGA2Boolean     true
GIS643_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefRECOVERY_UNDER_OVER_VOLTAGELOW,GLOBALA,ENTRY_VCUDTC_ENABLE_CRITERIA - All DTC's should not set within 5 seconds of a under or over voltage condition. _MQSRkEfUEeW-6Omy13RgGA2Integer 9161.0none9
GIS643_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDTC_IGN_CYCLE_CTR_MAXLOW,GLOBALA,ENTRY_VCUTODO - Add a description _dtB1QUfUEeW-6Omy13RgGA1Integer 0401.0none0
GIS643_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDID_MASK_2FLOW,GLOBALA,ENTRY_VCUDID $2F  VEHICLEIDENTIFICATIONNUMBER DIGITS 2-17. This DID contains the Vehicle Identification Number. The data content shall be specified by the vehicle manufacturer. If the VIN has not yet been programmed into the ECU's permanent memory, those locations shall be either '$00' or '$FF'. _pPZsQUfUEeW-6Omy13RgGA1Boolean     false
GIS643_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDID_B6_BYTE_1LOW,GLOBALA,ENTRY_VCUDID_B6_BYTE_1_MODEL_YEAR - This byte is an USN value that represents the target model year using the year 1900 as the base year. To represent the 2000 model year, the value of byte 1 would be 100 or ($64). This is derived from 1900 + 100 = 2000. If the module supports multiple model years, then this byte is filled in with $23 (ASCII equivalent to #).GMW 3110 Appendice C_vZJRsUfUEeW-6Omy13RgGA1Integer -214748364821474836471.0none0
GIS643_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDID_B6_BYTE_2LOW,GLOBALA,ENTRY_VCUDID_B6_BYTE_2_VEHICLE_MAKE - This byte is the ASCII representation of the Vehicle Make as Identified in the Vehicle Identification Number (VIN). For example, Cadillac = 6 or $36. If the part is used in multiple vehicle makes, then a value of $23 (ASCII #) is used. GMW 3110 Appendice C_v74yYEfUEeW-6Omy13RgGA1String  100 UTF8TODO
GIS643_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDID_B6_BYTE_3LOW,GLOBALA,ENTRY_VCUDID_B6_BYTE_3_VEHICLE_CAR_LINE - This byte is an ASCII character representing the vehicle car line (e.g., J for Chevy Cavalier). If the module can be used in multiple car lines, then a value of $23 (ASCII ???#???) is used.GMW 3110 Appendice C_wYIpgUfUEeW-6Omy13RgGA1String  100 UTF8TODO
GIS643_DiagnosticCalDef_GlobalA.caldefDID_B6_BYTE_4_5LOW,GLOBALA,ENTRY_VCUDID_B6_BYTE_4_5_ENGINE_RPO - Bytes 4 and 5 contain the ASCII representation of the last two characters of the engine Regular Production Option (RPO). The first character of an engine RPO is constant and is therefore not stored. For the L67 engine, the value for these two bytes would be 6 = $36, and 7 = $37. If the module is to be used with multiple engines, then a $23 (ASCII #) is put in both bytes. If the module can be used with any engine type, then $2A (ASCII *) is put in both bytes. GMW 3110 Appendice C_zFtqEUfUEeW-6Omy13RgGA1String  100 UTF8TODO

GIS651_TimeOfDay.caldefTCPLOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUThe Low Radio shall utilize the TCP calibration to conclude which scenario, described in the table shown below, applies to the Low Radio, as follows: If TCP == TRUE, the System Time is synchronized to the trusted time source, GPS time provided by the TCP module. The ClockOffset is used for determination of the Time of Day message. If TCP == FALSE, there is no trusted time source for System Time to synchronize. The ClockOffset shall be set to zero and not used for the determination of the Time of Day message. Changes to the Manual Clock time shall be reflected in the System Time maintained by the Low Radio.GIS-651-255 ~MY18 System Time for Low Radio_wvZsQKAMEea-Ao5FQchwtQ4Boolean     false
GIS651_TimeOfDay.caldefTZDSTAckWaitTimeMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUWait time to acknowledge a time zone or day light savings time change beforeTOD provider recalculates the ClockOffset and updates the Time of Day message.GIS-651-310_9tqw4HGkEeSbMrVThuMbXA3Integer 010231.0seconds600
GIS651_TimeOfDay.caldefGPS_DATA_SOURCELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUCalibration to determine whether GPS/UTC Time is a potential time source for synchronization.GIS-651-130_mSCtoFz3EeOQzJtmpqqaoA3Enumeration     ExternalOnStarGPS
GIS651_TimeOfDay.caldefPI_CODE_CONFIGURATIONLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUFor setting the PI code for the countries - Definition is $HighByteLowByte, eg. Germany = $2002GIS-651-245_v3EuMFz3EeOQzJtmpqqaoA3Integer 0655351.0none0
GIS651_TimeOfDay.caldefRDS_CLOCK_TIME_MAX_DIFFLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUCalibration to determine whether received RDS Clock Time should be used for synchronization. The TOD provider compares the current time to the received RDS clock time and checks whether the received RDS clock time differs from RDS_CLOCK_TIME_MAX_DIFF calibration (DEFAULT=5min). If difference is greater than the calibration value, clock will not be synchronized! If difference is less than calibration value, then clock will be synchronized GIS-651-245_8zzqYFz3EeOQzJtmpqqaoA3Integer 0151.0none5
GIS651_TimeOfDay.caldefTZDSTAdjust_LLMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCULower limit value used to recognize either Daylight Savings Time or Time Zone change for cellular network time synchronization source.GIS-651-225_SCSAwLpiEeOPlcP4_0xCtQ3Integer 040961.0seconds1800
GIS651_TimeOfDay.caldefTZDSTAdjust_ULMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUUpper limit value used to recognize either Daylight Savings Time or Time Zone change for cellular network time synchronization source.GIS-651-225_QJ0N0LpjEeOPlcP4_0xCtQ3Integer 040961.0seconds3600

GIS653_AMFMTunerCommon.caldefPhaseDiversityEnabledMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration defines whether or not the tuner shall support phase diversity. TRUE means phase diveristy shall be enabled in the tuner. FALSE means that phase diversity is disabled in the tuner. The default state is FALSE. _oMWq8K3xEeSNHqFaWuW3xA3Boolean     false
GIS653_AMFMTunerCommon.caldefAM_FM_FastSeekRateLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis AM_FM_FastTuneRate defines the Fast Tune Rate in frequency steps per second. This calibration is used when the User executes an AM or FM Fast Tune operation. The default value is 10 frequency steps/sec. _BuMdIdeAEeOGn-iEWDtkAA4Integer 5201.0none10
GIS653_AMFMTunerCommon.caldefAM_FM_FastTuneRateLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis AM_FM_FastTuneRate defines the Fast Tune Rate in frequency steps per second. This calibration is used when the User executes an AM or FM Fast Tune operation. The default value is 10 frequency steps/sec. _qlbKwNd_EeOGn-iEWDtkAA4Integer 5201.0none10
GIS653_AMFMTunerCommon.caldefFaderPeriodLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration defines the total timing (cycle time) of the muting process in msec. The default value is 50msec. _ypiV2k1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Float 0.02000.00.1milliseconds50.0
GIS653_AMFMTunerCommon.caldefAntennaFailCheckIntervalLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUAfter switching off the antenna in case of overcurrent protection the antenna supply is switches on again within an AntennaFailRecheckInterval which could by calibrated by CAL_AntennaFailRecheckInterval. _ypi84E1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Integer 10040001.0none250
GIS653_AMTuner.caldefBrazilAMBandDisableLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis cal BrazilAMBandDisable for disabling AM band in Brazil and also to disable AM Band for other markets, if required.When enabled, the AM Band source icon supporting AM shall be removed.GIS-653-7600_SXXSERt1EeWzztLp5T7pOw3Boolean     false
GIS653_AMTuner.caldefAntennaConfigurationLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUhis calibration informs the tuner whether a mast antenna is included as part of the Infotainment configuration. Only configurations with the mast antenna option are required to run the Antenna Mast Installation Verification test. If True, includes mast antenna. If false, doesn't include mast antenna. Default is False.GIS-653-7200_4hHxABttEeWeKtb2c1Y4bg3Boolean     false

GIS653_AMTuner.caldefSS_AM_IF_BandwidthControlParameterMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration defines the AM IF Bandwidth parameter in kHz. The default value is set to Auto (0). This is a Supplier Specific Calibration. _3Qo8Ia42EeSPIs8u9gqkmw2Integer 0311.0none1
GIS653_AMTuner.caldefSS_AM_SoftMuteLimiterMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration defines the AM Soft Mute Limiter parameter in dB. The default value is 5 (-30dB). This is a Supplier Specific Calibration. The integer value selected defines the dB setting. For further details can be found in the SAF775X Radio User Manual Section 7.11.6.  _o8A_8a42EeSPIs8u9gqkmw2Integer 0151.0none5
GIS653_AMTuner.caldefSS_AM_SoftMuteSlopeMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration defines the AM Soft Mute Slope parameter in dB. The default value is 0 (30dB). This is a Supplier Specific Calibration. The integer value selected defines the dB setting. For further details can be found in the SAF775X Radio User Manual Section 7.11.4.  _Og2vwa42EeSPIs8u9gqkmw2Integer 071.0none0
GIS653_AMTuner.caldefSS_AM_SoftMuteStartMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration defines the AM Soft Mute Start parameter in dBµV. The integer value selected defines the dBµV setting. For further details can be found in the SAF775X Radio User Manual Section 7.11.4. The default value is 5 (26dBµV). This is a Supplier Specific Calibration.SAF775X Radio User Manual Section 7.11.4_dVO_gK41EeSPIs8u9gqkmw3Integer 0151.0none5
GIS653_AMTuner.caldefSS_AM_SoftMuteResponseMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis calibration selects a Slow or Fast response for AM Soft Muting. The default value is Slow. This is a Supplier Specific Calibration. _SC1ToK40EeSPIs8u9gqkmw3Enumeration     Slow
GIS653_AMTuner.caldefAntennaPhantomPowerCycleTimeLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUCreates a cycle time for toggling the phantom power On-Off time as defined in specific profile of conducting this test. Cycles times will be on the order of msecs. For example if this cal is set to 200 msecs, it means the tuner shall apply phantom power for 200 msec and then immediately turn the phantom power off.GIS-653-7500_HsQB8RtvEeWeKtb2c1Y4bg3Integer 030001.0milliseconds200
GIS653_AMTuner.caldefAM_TimeToFirstAudioLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration defines the time in msec to delay producing audio after a preset selection, seek/tune, or direct tune process before audio is played to allow for system dynamic switching conditions to dissipate. See GMW16273. _M2ufYHokEeSjYPic2Gk-6g3Integer 036001.0milliseconds250

GIS653_AMTuner.caldefAM_SeekStopCountLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration sets the minimum SeekStops that must occur to trigger the pass-fail test result. If the SeekStop count exceeds the AM_SeekStopCount calibration, then the test passes. If the SeekStop count is less that the AM_SeekStopCount calibration, then the test fails.GIS-653-7200_rzA8IRtsEeWeKtb2c1Y4bg3Integer 0601.0none25
GIS653_AMTuner.caldefAM_AntMastSignalThresholdLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration sets the signal threshold for determining the presence of an AM strong station during an Update Station list sequence. The value of this calibration may or may not be the same as the cal used for AM strong station thresholds used during normal tuning operations. This provides more flexibility for tailoring the test criteria independently of the strong station threshold used in the production calibration.GIS-653-7200_VGRU4RtgEeWeKtb2c1Y4bg3Integer 0601.0none25

GIS653_FMTuner.caldefAF_Min_FieldStrengthLevelLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUIf Fieldstrength is higher than level then PI check is allowed. Optional Calibration - Supplier Dependent. _yrRbMU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 0601.0none20
GIS653_FMTuner.caldefAF_QualityLOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUQuality threshold for AF switching. Optional Calibration - Supplier Dependent.GIS-338-202 RDS Preset Recall_ypBYc01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Integer 0151.0none10
GIS653_FMTuner.caldefDeviationThresholdNeighborStationLOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUIf the deviation of the current tuned station exceeds this level neighbor channel distortions will be less considered. Optional Calibration - Supplier Dependent. For MID, HIGH: This cann be adjusted by 2 values: BWS Automatic bandwidth control sensitivity _yqR8s01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 02551.0none32
GIS653_FMTuner.caldefDirectTuneAllEnabledLOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUThe DirectTuneAllEnabled calibration when TRUE, allows the tuner to receive and process (i.e., tune to) any frequency, regardless of the internal tuner frequency step size. If this calibration is set to FALSE, the tuner shall only process Direct Tune entries that align with the internal tuner frequency step size. The provides the capability to tune to any theoretical frequency within a given frequency range when a market requires this support. The default state for this calibration is FALSE.GIS-653-2315 AM/FM Direct Tune Function_ULdIcM_GEeObGfP4S25hHw3Boolean     false
GIS653_FMTuner.caldefEON_TAHoldLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUAfter losing RDS-synchronization it is assumed that the TA is still active for the defined time (used for TA and EON-TA). If this time expires the TA is cancelled or a EON return takes place. Applicable to All SuppliersGIS-338-206A TP Volume_yq1WUU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 102401.0milliseconds120

GIS653_FMTuner.caldefFMSingleTunerEnableLOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUThis calibration controls the tuner operation in the Low Radio. Between True (single tuner mode) and False (dual tuner mode). FM stations with RDS-synchronization above FM Memory Scan Level (FM_MemoryScanLevel) shall always be tuned by the memory scan.GIS-338-302 FM Memory Scan, GIS-338-201 RDS Acceptance Criteria_yq3yoU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Integer 0601.0none36

GIS653_FMTuner.caldefFM_OffsetLOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUThis calibration defines the general FM offset for quality in/out for the center frequency of a FM station. GIS653_FMTuner.caldefFM_Quality_ThresholdLOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUThreshold value of averaged FM quality to rate a good FM receptionGIS-653-2200 AM/FM Station List and Strong Station Lists_ypnOUk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw4Integer 0101.0none2
GIS653_FMTuner.caldefFM_RDS_AF_switching_enabledLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis boolean parameter defines whether the RDS alternate frequency switching is enabled. This allows a receiver to re-tune to a different frequency providing the same station when the first signal becomes too weak (e.g., when moving out of range). This is often utilized in car stereo systems.GIS-338-101A FM-RDS Functionality_V06v4AOAEeWVjPovnrTcnw3Boolean     false
GIS653_FMTuner.caldefFM_RDS_Clocktime_enabledLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis boolean parameter defines whether the RDS clocktime feature is enabled. The feature allows to synchronize a clock in the receiver or the main clock in a car. Due to transmission vagaries, CT can only be accurate to within 100 ms of UTC.GIS-338-102D PTY 31_DISKQQODEeWVjPovnrTcnw3Boolean     false
GIS653_FMTuner.caldefFM_RDS_EON_enabledLOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUThis boolean parameter defines whether the RDS enhanced other networks feature is enabled. The feature allows the receiver to monitor other networks or stations for traffic programmes, and automatically temporarily tune into that station.GIS-338-101A FM-RDS Functionality_wMSsQQOAEeWVjPovnrTcnw4Boolean     false
GIS653_FMTuner.caldefFM_RDS_PI_ignoreLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis boolean parameter defines whether the PI code should be ignored or not. Semantics: "true: station list is only frequency oriented, to prevent problems with different stations using same PI (relevant for China only); false: normal behavior "GIS-338-101A FM-RDS Functionality_5d1ToQOBEeWVjPovnrTcnw3Boolean     false
GIS653_FMTuner.caldefFM_RDS_PSN_RT_enabledLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration parameter enables the RDS program service name (PSN) and Radio Text (RT) feature in the tuner. If set to false, the tuner shall display only the frequency.  _n9BPwDMjEeaWo8FF24C81A3Boolean     false
GIS653_FMTuner.caldefFM_RDS_PTY31_enabledLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis boolean parameter defines whether the program type 31 alarm is used. RDS defines a coding of up to 31 pre-defined programme types (e.g., in Europe: PTY1 News, PTY6 Drama, PTY11 Rock music) allows users to find similar programming by genre. PTY31 seems to be reserved for emergency announcements in the event of natural disasters or other major calamities. Semantics: true: PTY31 alarm announcement activeGIS-338-102D PTY 31_km6SoQOCEeWVjPovnrTcnw3Boolean     false
GIS653_FMTuner.caldefFM_RDS_PTY_enabledLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThic cal enable or disables the FM RDS program type feature. If set to false the RDS PTY data is not processed by the tuner. _jFeIUDIuEea7c5H_UHgXVQ3Boolean     false
GIS653_FMTuner.caldefFM_RDS_REG_switching_enabledLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis boolean parameter defines whether the RDS region switching feature is enabled. It is mainly used in countries where national broadcasters run "region-specific" programming such as regional opt-outs on some of their transmitters. This functionality allows the user to "lock-down" the set to their current region or let the radio tune into other region-specific programming as they move into the other region."GIS-338-406A TP setting off / REG setting is off" and subsequent sections_VIigIQOCEeWVjPovnrTcnw3Boolean     false
GIS653_FMTuner.caldefFM_RDS_TMC_enabledLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis boolean parameter defines whether the RDS traffic message channel feature is enabled.GIS-338-101A FM-RDS Functionality_1ZvokQOAEeWVjPovnrTcnw3Boolean     false
GIS653_FMTuner.caldefFM_RDS_TP_enabledLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis boolean parameter defines whether the RDS traffic program feature is enabled.GIS-338-101A FM-RDS Functionality_exr2kQOAEeWVjPovnrTcnw3Boolean     false

GIS653_FMTuner.caldefTA_UserNeutralPositionLOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUGIS-68A-206A: actual Volume used for a traffic announcements influenced by TA_OFFSET Cal and neutral position for User HMI setting _ysFTgU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 12531.0none24
GIS653_FMTuner.caldefTP_LostHoldTimeLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUIf TP function is activated and the actual TP station is below TP Quality Level for more than TP_LostHoldTime, the radio will look for a TP station in FM Memory which is above TP Quality Level, if no TP station is found in FM Memory start seek for a TP station in entire band which is above TP_SeekLevel, but only if radio is not the active If TP function is activated and the actual TP station is below TP Quality Level for more than TP_LostHoldTime, the radio will look for a TP station in FM Memory which is above TP Quality Level, if no TP station is found in FM Memory start seek for a TP station in entire band which is above TP_SeekLevel, but only if radio is not the active source or when volume 0 is selected.GIS-338-206B TP Seek_ysJk_U1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 102401.0none180
GIS653_FMTuner.caldefTP_QualityLevelLOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUIf TP function is activated and the actual TP station is below TP Quality Level for more than TP_LostHoldTime, the radio will look for a TP station in FM Memory which is above TP Quality Level, if no TP station is found in FM Memory start seek for a TP station in entire band which is above TP_SeekLevel, but only if radio is not the active If TP function is activated and the actual TP station is below TP Quality Level for more than TP_LostHoldTime, the radio will look for a TP station in FM Memory which is above TP Quality Level, if no TP station is found in FM Memory start seek for a TP station in entire band which is above TP_SeekLevel, but only if radio is not the active source or when volume 0 is selected.GIS-338-206B TP Seek_ysJk_k1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 02551.0none128
GIS653_FMTuner.caldefTP_SeekLevelLOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUThis will be used if the User initiates a TP seek. _ysJk_01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 0601.0none25

GIS655_DABTuner.caldefDAB_SlideShowMinDisplayTimeLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe cal_SlideShowMinDisplayTime shall establish a miniumum display time for an image within the HMI display.GIS-316-3115 Album Art and Station Logos (Slideshow), GIS-502-4.1.68.1*MY18 Slideshow_GocLYNK8EeS7moXKCsUKKg3Integer 5301.0seconds15
GIS655_DABTuner.caldefDAB_DAB_LinkingActiveLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe DAB_DAB_LinkingActive cal defines whether DAB to DAB linking shall be enabled in the tuner and whether or not the associated HMI menu item(s) associated with this feature should be displayed. TRUE means enable DAB to DAB linking in the tuner and display the associated DAB to DAB Linking menu item(s). FALSE means disable DAB to DAB linking in the tuner and disable HMI display of the associated DAB to DAB Linking menu item(s). The default value is TRUE. The tuner shall also take appropriate action to support/not support the processing of this feature based on the cal setting. This helps balance and optimize system resources based on activated features for a given market.GIS-699-031900 DAB Default Settings_94SNIScAEeSmb8tw1ZVRMw3Boolean     true
GIS655_DABTuner.caldefDAB_FM_LinkingActiveLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe DAB_FM_LinkingActive cal defines whether DAB to FM linking shall be enabled in the tuner and whether or not the associated HMI menu item(s) associated with this feature should be displayed. TRUE means enable DAB to FM linking in the tuner and display the associated DAB to FM Linking menu item(s). FALSE means disable DAB to FM linking in the tuner and disable HMI display of the associated DAB to FM Linking menu item(s). The default value is TRUE. The tuner shall also take appropriate action to support/not support the processing of this feature based on the cal setting. This helps balance and optimize system resources based on activated features for a given market.GIS-699-031900 DAB Default Settings_5AkQQScAEeSmb8tw1ZVRMw3Boolean     true
GIS655_DABTuner.caldefDAB_AnnouncementsActiveLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe DAB_AnnouncementsActive cal defines whether the HMI menu items associated with DAB Announcements and the associated tuner processing should be enabled/disabled for a specific region. TRUE means display the DAB announcements HMI menu item(s) and activate DAB announcement processing in the Tuner. FALSE means do not display the DAB announcements HMI menu item(s) and do not activate DAB announcement processing in the tuner. The default value is TRUE. This helps balance and optimize system resources based on features activated for a given market.GIS-699-031900 DAB Default Settings_v_sEcSbmEeSmb8tw1ZVRMw3Boolean     false
GIS655_DABTuner.caldefDAB_EnableDABLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe DAB_EnableDAB calibration defines whether all HMI menu items associated with DAB and the associated DAB tuner processing should be enabled/disabled for a specific region. TRUE means display enabled DAB HMI menu item(s) and activate DAB processing in the Tuner. FALSE means do not display any DAB related HMI menu item(s) and do not activate DAB processing in the tuner. The default value is TRUE. The tuner shall also take appropriate action to support/not support the processing of this feature based on the cal setting. This helps balance and optimize system resources based on activated features for a given market.GIS-699-031000 DAB Activation_yqq-RU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Boolean     true
GIS655_DABTuner.caldefDAB_EnableAlbumArtLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe DAB_EnableAlbumArt calibration defines whether the HMI menu item associated with DAB_EnableAlbumArt and the associated tuner processing should be enabled/disabled for a specific region. TRUE means display the DAB_EnableAlbumArt HMI menu item(s) and activate DAB_EnableAlbumArt processing in the Tuner. FALSE means do not display the DAB_EnableAlbumArt HMI menu item(s) and do not activate DAB_EnableAlbumArt processing in the tuner. The default value is TRUE. The tuner shall also take appropriate action to support/not support the processing of this feature based on the cal setting. This helps balance and optimize system resources based on activated features for a given market.GIS-699-030810 Album Art and Station Logos (Slideshow)_AcMSYRp3EeWoercuE1M_bg3Boolean     true
GIS655_DABTuner.caldefDAB_EnableSlideShowLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe DAB_EnableSlideShow calibration defines whether the HMI menu item associated with DAB_EnableSlideShow and the associated tuner processing should be enabled/disabled for a specific region. TRUE means display the DAB_EnableSlideShow HMI menu item(s) and activate DAB_EnableSlideShow processing in the Tuner. FALSE means do not display the DAB_EnableSlideShow HMI menu item(s) and do not activate DAB_EnableSlideShow processing in the tuner. The default value is TRUE. A False setting helps balance system resources by removing tuner processing of this feature and the associated HMI.GIS-699-030810 Album Art and Station Logos (Slideshow)_XWpDgNK7EeS7moXKCsUKKg3Boolean     true
GIS655_DABTuner.caldefDAB_DABFrequencyBandLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUCalibration parameter for selecting the DAB frequency band.GIS-313-1410 Frequency Bands_TxhEANLIEeS7moXKCsUKKg3Enumeration     Band_III_EU
GIS655_DABTuner.caldefDAB_EnableEPGMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThe DAB_EnableEPG calibration defines whether HMI menu items associated with DAB EPG and the associated DAB EPG tuner processing should be enabled/disabled for a specific region. TRUE means display enabled DAB EPG HMI menu item(s) and activate DAB EPG processing in the Tuner. FALSE means do not display any DAB EPG related HMI menu item(s) and do not activate DAB EPG processing in the tuner. The default value is FALSE. The tuner shall also take appropriate action to support/not support the processing of this feature based on the cal setting. This helps balance and optimize system resources based on activated features for a given market.GIS-316-3300 Electronic Program Guide _krjc8NbeEeSNzKzbjZ2dbQ2Boolean     false
GIS655_DABTuner.caldefDAB_EnableTPEGMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThe DAB_EnableTPEG calibration defines whether HMI menu items associated with DAB TPEG and the associated DAB TPEG tuner processing should be enabled/disabled for a specific region. TRUE means display enabled DAB TPEG HMI menu item(s) and activate DAB TPEG processing in the Tuner. FALSE means do not display any DAB TPEG related HMI menu item(s) and do not activate DAB TPEG processing in the tuner. The default value is TRUE. The tuner shall also take appropriate action to support/not support the processing of this feature based on the cal setting. This helps balance and optimize system resources based on activated features for a given market.GIS-316-7600 TPEG Real Time Traffic Services_v8XX8NbgEeSNzKzbjZ2dbQ2Boolean     false
GIS655_DABTuner.caldefDAB_DefaultChannelLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe default T-DAB Channel is 5A. This value can be calibrated by the parameter DAB_DefaultChannel. GIS-316-3511 Initial activation (default settings)_DdNjcNOWEeSPn-nUOvTPiQ3Enumeration     DABChannels_5A
GIS655_DABTuner.caldefDAB_DABMuteOffLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUTo improve the User Experience, a calibration defined as cal_DABMuteOn and cal_DABMuteOff will be used to create a mute/unmute ramp time when a DAB signal is acquired.GIS-313-1920 Acquisition time after changing preset memory_NDtVIdL9EeSPn-nUOvTPiQ3Integer 10020001.0milliseconds500
GIS655_DABTuner.caldefDAB_DABMuteOnLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUTo improve the User Experience, a calibration defined as cal_DABMuteOn and cal_DABMuteOff will be used to create a mute/unmute ramp time when a DAB signal is acquired.GIS-313-1920 Acquisition time after changing preset memory_9-Gn8NL8EeSPn-nUOvTPiQ3Integer 10020001.0milliseconds500
GIS655_DABTuner.caldefDAB_DABAntennaFailCheckIntervalLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUAfter switching off the antenna in case of overcurrent protection, the antenna supply shall switch back on again within an antenna fail recheck interval. This interval shall be set by the cal_DABAntennaFailCheckInterval defined in the DABTuner.caldef file in GIS-646. GIS-313-1300 Active Antenna Power Supply (Phantom supply)_uky4cNL4EeSPn-nUOvTPiQ3Integer 5301.0seconds10
GIS655_DABTuner.caldefDAB_SeekStopBIIILOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe following parameters define the FIC-EFC (or FIC-BIR) which has to be reached to judge sufficient DAB signal available in DAB software. It shall be used for any kind of seeking to ultimately add an ensemble to a station list. A calibration defined as CAL_SeekStopBIII shall be used to define Tuning Stop Sensitivity.GIS-313-1510 Tuning Stop Sensitivity_q-N94NL5EeSPn-nUOvTPiQ3Float 5.0255.00.001none50.999
GIS655_DABTuner.caldefDAB_FastSeekTuneRateLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe DAB_FastSeekTuneRate is a combined Seek/Tune cal that defines the Fast Seek/Tune Rate in steps per second for DAB tuning operations. This calibration is used when the User executes an DAB Fast Seek/Tune operation. The default value is 3 steps/sec. _4IlYUNeAEeOGn-iEWDtkAA3Integer 2101.0none3
GIS655_DABTuner.caldefDAB_UserSetting_AnnouncementFilterLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis function can be configured by selecting the corresponding Announcement type in the AnnouncementFilter flag field parameter. The default value can be calibrated by the CAL_UserSetting_AnnouncementFilter. Alarm Announcements are always enabled and cannot be deactivated by user settings.GIS-316-4830 User Setting  Announcement Filter_5hcEkNOaEeSDguH-pictGw3Enumeration     DABAnnouncementFilter_Alarm

Global_Info3.caldefENABLE_EMBEDDED_CONTENTMID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALBThe following Boolean Parameter ENABLE_EMBEDDED_CONTENT tells the system that embedded content is available for consumption. This Boolean parameter Enables (i.e. when value = true)/Disables (i.e. when value = false). This is required because we want the ability to enable/disable embedded content sources. FNC 805741 Media Player Requirements in DNG_qURRMYDJEeqhy4P-28qx7w1Boolean     false

HMIExternalVehicleFeatures.caldefMWS_PANE_PASSENGER_VIEW_DELAYMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUMini Mode also allows for both the driver and the passenger to be adjusting their seats simultaneously. The Driver always gets priority in controlling their seat. If the Passenger is controlling their seat and the driver begins to play with their seat control the Driver seat would shift to the 3/4 view and the passenger would go to mini mode. Figure 9.18.8.1 shows the passenger with primary control. If the Driver operates the switch the view would switch to give the Driver screen dominance as shown in Figure 9.18.8.2 If the Driver completes their interaction after 3 seconds (calibratable) when their pane times out the Passenger would regain the dominant view. _yrTQa01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 0255001.0none3000
HMIExternalVehicleFeatures.caldefMWS_PANE_AUTO_DISMISS_TIMERMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUMULTIWAYSEAT_STATUS_PANE_AUTO_DISMISS_TIMER - calibration to set the time period to wait before auto-dismissing the Seat Status Pane due to inactivity with the seat switch controls. Type - ENM Range = 0-255 Sec Resolution = 1 Sec Default = 3 Sec.  _yrTQbE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 02551.0none3
HMIExternalVehicleFeatures.caldefMWS_PANE_MINI_MODE_TIMERMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUMULTIWAYSEAT_STATUS_PANE_MINI_MODE_TIMER - calibration to set the time period to wait before switching the dominant view (i.e. Driver OR Passenger) in the Miniature Seat Status Pane (Mini Mode). Type - ENM Range = 0-255 Sec Resolution = 1 Sec Default = 3 Sec. _yrTQbU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 02551.0none3
HMIExternalVehicleFeatures.caldefMWS_PANE_SUPPRESSION_TIMERMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUMULTIWAYSEAT_STATUS_PANE_SUPPRESSION_TIMER - calibration to set the time period to wait after the Seat Status Pane is suppressed into mini mode before returning back to full screen mode. Type - ENM Range = 0-255 Sec Resolution = 1 Sec Default = 20 Sec. _yrTQbk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 02551.0none20

LanguageAndRegionalizationGlobalA.caldefAVAILABLE_TARGET_SYSTEM_LANGUAGE_ENABLESLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration is intended to be used to enable (display in the SETTINGS SYSTEM LANGUAGE selection menu list)/disable (not display in the SETTINGS SYSTEM LANGUAGE selection menu list) as needed the available system languages in the SETTINGS SYSTEM LANGUAGE selection menu. A given system language shall be displayed in the SETTINGS SYSTEM LANGUAGE selection menu list when the enable calibration for that system language is set to a value of 1 (enable), and shall not be displayed when the enable calibration for that system language is set to a value of 0 (disable).GIS-507 and GIS-547_X7ucgDisEeaLbPZ4FkO9uQ3EnumIntegerMap 011.0noneENGLISH_US_ENABLED:1
FRENCH_CANADIAN_ENABLED:1
SPANISH_NORTH_AMERICAN_ENABLED:1
GERMAN_ENABLED:0
ENGLISH_UK_ENABLED:0
ITALIAN_ENABLED:0
SPANISH_ENABLED:0
FRENCH_ENABLED:0
CHINESE_SIMPLIFIED_ENABLED:0
RUSSIAN_ENABLED:0
DUTCH_ENABLED:0
TURKISH_ENABLED:0
POLISH_ENABLED:0
PORTUGUESE_ENABLED:0
KOREAN_ENABLED:0
ARABIC_ENABLED:0
GREEK_ENABLED:0
SWEDISH_ENABLED:0
ENGLISH_AUSTRALIAN_ENABLED:0
HUNGARIAN_ENABLED:0
PORTUGUESE_BRAZILIAN_ENABLED:0
ENGLISH_SOUTH_AFRICA_ENABLED:0
DANISH_ENABLED:0
ROMANIAN_ENABLED:0
NORWEGIAN_ENABLED:0
FINNISH_ENABLED:0
CROATION_ENABLED:0
SLOVENIAN_ENABLED:0
ENGLISH_INDIA_ENABLED:0
THAI_ENABLED:0
CZECH_ENABLED:0
SLOVAK_ENABLED:0
BULGARIAN_ENABLED:0
UKRAINIAN_ENABLED:0
SERBIAN_ENABLED:0
JAPANESE_ENABLED:0
LanguageAndRegionalizationGlobalA.caldefDEFAULT_SYSTEM_LANGUAGELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThis calibration shall be used to set the initial default system language that is available in the SETTINGS SYSTEM LANGUAGE selection menu. The DEFAULT_SYSTEM_LANGUAGE shall be checked to confirm it is one of the available system languages enabled at a given time by the TARGET_SYSTEM_LANGUAGE_ENABLE_CALIBRATIONS. If at any time the value set for the DEFAULT_SYSTEM_LANGUAGE calibration is not one of the available system languages enabled by the TARGET_SYSTEM_LANGUAGE_ENABLE_CALIBRATIONS, the DEFAULT_SYSTEM_LANGUAGE calibration shall be set to a fail safe value based on the value the REGION calibration is set to at that time. For the purpose of referencing the REGION calibration to determine the fail safe value to set the DEFAULT_SYSTEM_LANGUAGE calibration if the REGION calibration is set to any of the following four REGION enumeration calibration values of 1.) EUROPE, 2.) AFRICA, 3.) AUSTRALIA, or 4.) INDIA, the DEFAULT_SYSTEM_LANGUAGE calibration shall be set to a fail safe default system language of ENGLISH_UK. Otherwise if the REGION calibration is not set to any one of these four Region enumeration calibration values, the DEFAULT_SYSTEM_LANGUAGE calibration shall be set to a fail safe default system language of ENGLISH_US.GIS-507 and GIS-547_E4ARALEQEeO_VpIpRoy4MQ4Enumeration     ENGLISH_US
LanguageAndRegionalizationGlobalA.caldefCOUNTRY_CODELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUUNITED STATES IS DEFAULT COUNTRY CODE. Contry code will be based on RPO Calibration. As referenced in the GIS-331 Wifi Requirements Specification, this country code calibration is also being used for determining the regulatory wifi power variation settings to use for the wifi chip that shall be dynamically determined and set based on the value (country value) set for this calibration. Note, the value (country value) set for this calibration is determined by the country RPO (Regular Production Options Content) code for a given vehicle based on the PPC (Program Planning Content) for that vehicle, which represents the intended country of sell for that vehicle.GIS-331_J8iWULEaEeO_VpIpRoy4MQ6Enumeration     UNITED_STATES
LanguageAndRegionalizationGlobalA.caldefDRIVER_SIDELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCULEFT HAND DRIVE IS DEFAULT. DRIVE_SIDE to comprehend vehicle configuration of Right Hand Drive (RHD) versus Left Hand Drive (LHD) with respect to GMLAN message location of driver front versus passenger front. Selection based on RPO Calibration. _uuK8EMPnEeOFYIr7JW1WWw3Enumeration     LHD
LanguageAndRegionalizationGlobalA.caldefREGIONSLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUThe contents of the Audio More Features may be based on Regions. _Q_tGINRQEeOBI_YIOwraKg3Enumeration     GMNA
