LowRadioSSTunerParameters.caldefSS_LOW_RADIO_TUNER_BINLOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUBinary of Low Radio Supplier Specific tuner parameters _wNticALZEeWhetwCpvIaHA3BinaryFile     LowRadioDefaultTunerData.bin[1392 bytes]:CRC=1393592315
MuteUnmuteRampTimes.caldefK_MUTEX_SINK_RAMP_3LOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUIn order to allow flexibility when using the mute/unmute function, three(3) calibratible ramp parameters are usable by the Audio Manager when commanding the Amplifier to mute or unmute the Mutex Sink. The ramp parameters are calibrations provided in the Audio Manager as numeric values between 0 and 1275 ms with a resolution of 5 ms. The calibrations shall be implemented within either the CSM or Low Radio depending on system configuration. The usage of the ramp rates shall be configurable according to which sources are former and current to produce desirable transitions in audio sources. GIS-604-655_ZJXTsIOlEeOYAdh1BRO4Xw4Integer 012755.0milliseconds10
MuteUnmuteRampTimes.caldefK_MUTEX_SINK_RAMP_2LOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUIn order to allow flexibility when using the mute/unmute function, three(3) calibratible ramp parameters are usable by the Audio Manager when commanding the Amplifier to mute or unmute the Mutex Sink. The ramp parameters are calibrations provided in the Audio Manager as numeric values between 0 and 1275 ms with a resolution of 5 ms. The calibrations shall be implemented within either the CSM or Low Radio depending on system configuration. The usage of the ramp rates shall be configurable according to which sources are former and current to produce desirable transitions in audio sources. GIS-604-655_VW7j4YOlEeOYAdh1BRO4Xw4Integer 012755.0milliseconds10
MuteUnmuteRampTimes.caldefK_MUTEX_SINK_RAMP_1LOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUIn order to allow flexibility when using the mute/unmute function, three(3) calibratible ramp parameters are usable by the Audio Manager when commanding the Amplifier to mute or unmute the Mutex Sink. The ramp parameters are calibrations provided in the Audio Manager as numeric values between 0 and 1275 ms with a resolution of 5 ms. The calibrations shall be implemented within either the CSM or Low Radio depending on system configuration. The usage of the ramp rates shall be configurable according to which sources are former and current to produce desirable transitions in audio sources. GIS-604-655_DM6DcIOlEeOYAdh1BRO4Xw4Integer 012755.0milliseconds10
NavApp.caldefEV_ENERGY_MODEL_PARAMETERSMID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALBThis calibration sets all the parametric EV vehicle data for the Navigation Vehicle Energy Model calculation that will determine the energy along the route and charging spot recommendations. It basically is used to calculate the range of the vehicle. This Calibration is actually a Blob that is composed of many individual parameters. The Blob composition will be further broken down in the functional requirements section. The software development team responsible for EV will need to create the Blob cal creation tools necessary.MY23 Nav Partner App FNC_sSDTUHkEEeqxUKJdn1c1Ew1BinaryFile     sample_blob_512kb[524000 bytes]:CRC=3612676257
NavApp.caldefEV_ENERGY_MODEL_FEATURE_MAPMID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALBThis EV calibration parameter includes a set of Boolean parameters that is configurable to individually Enables/Disables the sending of this data to the VEM Model. If the value is set to "0": the Nav System will not send the data to the VEM. If the value is set to "1": the Nav System will send the data to the VEM. _oR8l0HkCEeqxUKJdn1c1Ew1EnumIntegerMap 011.0noneEV_MASS:1
EV_BATTERY_LEVEL_WH:1
EV_BATTERY_CAPACITY_WH:1
EV_HVAC_STATUS:1
EV_REFERENCE_AIR_DENSITY:1
EV_EXTERNAL_TEMP_CELSIUS:1
EV_CONSTANT_ROLLING_RESISTANCE_F0:1
EV_LINEAR_ROTATIONAL_LOSSES_F1:1
EV_QUADRATIC_COEFFICIENT_OF_AIR_DRAG_F2:1
EV_DRIVETRAIN_POWER_LOSS:1
EV_BATTERY_LOSSES:1
EV_REGENERATION_RATE_AT_ENERGY_LEVEL:1
EV_REGENERATION_RATE_AT_TIME:1
EV_AUXILIARY_DRAIN_AT_TIME:1
EV_AUXILIARY_DRAIN_AT_TEMP:1
EV_CHARGING_CURVES:1
EV_TARGET_BATTERY_LEVEL_WH:1
EV_TIME_REMAINING_SECS:1
NavApp.caldefHD_MAP_AND_ROUTE_TYPEMID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALBThe use case of this calibration is intended to display Super Cruise/Ultra Cruise map and route where applicable. This calibration shall be set to the following: "0=None when SuperCruise or UltraCruise is not applicable", 1=SuperCruise for RPO: UKL, and "2=UltraCruise for RPO: UKR"Automotive Service Solution Function (Reference Module in DNG)_lubiQACHEeqcM4o___siSA1Enumeration     None
NavApp.caldefDEFAULT_POI_FUEL_STATIONMID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALA,GLOBALBDefault Fuel type POI search base on vehicle configuration. Also note the condition criteria for this calibration being enabled is the ENABLE_APPLICATION_NAVIGATION in the Global_Info3.caldef file within the Cal database. When, the ENABLE_APPLICATION_NAVIGATION calibration is set to true this calibration is enable else it is not. [ Old MY20 NAV ] GIS-504 Navigation UI Specification - Form and Behavior, GIS-620 Navigation Functional Requirements_3GQ9gMZaEeO9-d-JqUwEvQ2Enumeration     Gasoline
NavApp.caldefECO_ROUTE_CURVEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALBCalibration represents the curves that define ECO routing performance and criteria base vehicle specific. Also, note the condition criteria for this calibration being enabled is the ENABLE_APPLICATION_NAVIGATION. Also, note the condition criteria for this calibration being enabled is the ENABLE_APPLICATION_NAVIGATION within the Global_Info3.caldef file in the Cal database. When, the ENABLE_APPLICATION_NAVIGATION calibration is set to true this calibration is enable else it is not. [ Old MY20 NAV ]; THIS CALIBRATION SHOULD NOT BE USED FOR APPLICATIONS USING GASGIS-504 Navigation UI Specification - Form and Behavior, GIS-620 Navigation Functional Requirements_XeJ64MTnEeOePMFnmOVt0g4Enumeration     ECO_ROUTE_CURVE_0
NavApp.caldefPAY_BROADCAST_TRAFFICMID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALA,GLOBALBThis CAL is intended to enable/disable Pay Broadcast Traffic Service (TMC and TPEG). When this CAL is set to 1 (TRUE), this service is enabled or supported. When this CAL is set to 0 (FALSE), this service is disabled or not supported. [ Old MY20 NAV ]; THIS CALIBRATION SHOULD NOT BE USED FOR APPLICATIONS USING GASGIS-620 Navigation Functional Requirements_tu3JkMqLEeW_5NzJEimJmA3Boolean     true
NavApp.caldefMODULE_ORIENTATIONMID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALA,GLOBALBThis calibration is used as a reference in a look up table to determine the correct packaging orientation of the module in the vehicle for the module delta height calculation in software. [ New MY21 NAV ]GIS-620 Navigation Functional Requirements_0PVWEOQtEeen_5uNNn90GA1Integer 0501.0none0
NavApp.caldefK_PREDICTIVE_CARDS_ENABLEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALBThis cal allows the Predictive Cards feature to be disabled or enabled. [ New MY21 NAV ]; THIS CALIBRATION SHOULD NOT BE USED FOR APPLICATIONS USING GASGIS-620 Navigation Functional Requirements, GIS-1004 Navigation UI Speicification - Form and Behavior_lx99EDnVEeefbJ5EEtYQTQ2Boolean     false
NavApp.caldefK_RANGE_PROJECTION_AND_SMART_ROUTING_TYPEMID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALA,GLOBALBThis cal allows the Navigation Range Projection and Smart Routing Type to be set. [ New MY21 NAV ]GIS-620 Navigation Functional Requirements, GIS-942 Augmented Reality System Requirements_qqr34Dp3Eeesh_Gsoan6Vg1Enumeration     NO_RANGE_PROJECTION_OR_SMART_ROUTING
NavApp.caldefK_STREETVIEW_DATA_ENABLEMID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALA,GLOBALBThis cal allows the Navigation Streetview feature to be disabled or enabled. [ New MY21 NAV ]; THIS CALIBRATION SHOULD NOT BE USED FOR APPLICATIONS USING GASGIS-620 Navigation Functional Requirements_qeiTgDnNEeefbJ5EEtYQTQ2Boolean     false
NavApp.caldefK_DESTINATION_HIGHLIGHT_ENABLEMID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALBThe AR System utilizes the K_DESTINATION_HIGHLIGHT_ENABLE calibratable Configuration parameter to disable or enable the AR "Destination Highlilghting" functionality. This feature can be turned off by this calibration if it is not functional at the time of vehicle production. This feature can also be turned off for non-Info3.5C vehicles that do not support Augmented Reality. [ New MY21 AR ]GIS-620 Navigation Functional Requirements, GIS-1064 Augmented Reality UI Specification - Form and Behavior_ITvs4DnHEeefbJ5EEtYQTQ1Boolean     true
NavApp.caldefK_DESTINATION_HIGHLIGHT_PIN_HEIGHT_FROM_GROUNDMID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALBThe AR System utilizes the K_DESTINATION_HIGHLIGHT_PIN_HEIGHT_FROM_GROUND calibratable Tuning parameter to establish the AR "Destination Highlighting Pin" overlay vertical positioning on the Front Camera Video image. [ New MY21 AR ]GIS-620 Navigation Functional Requirements, GIS-1064 Augmented Reality UI Specification - Form and Behavior_HCXeIDqDEeesh_Gsoan6Vg1Integer 110000.1m40
NavApp.caldefK_DESTINATION_VIEW_ANGLE_HORIZONTALMID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALBThe AR System utilizes the K_DESTINATION_HIGHLIGHT_VIEW_HORIZONTAL calibratable Tuning parameter to establish a Destination Highlighting Pin display criteria. NavApp.caldefK_DYNAMIC_ARROW_ON_PATH_OVERLAY_ENABLEMID,HIGH,VCU,GLOBALBThe AR System utilizes the K_DYNAMIC_ARROW_ON_PATH_ENABLE calibratable Configuration parameter to disable or enable the the AR "Dynamic Arrow on Path" functionality. This feature can be turned off by this calibration if it is not functional at the time of vehicle production. This feature can also be turned off for non-Info3.5C vehicles that do not support Augmented Reality. SFS2_SecurityExtensionsFRD.caldefDEVICE_REGISTRATION_ENABLE_CHECK_OVERRIDEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUFlag defined for development purposes to bypass the Manufacturing Enable Counter checks that inhibit automatic device registration until the vehicle has been fully assembled and tested. This flag is intended to be left at its FALSE value for production release. When TRUE, automatic device registration will be enabled irrespective of the MEC. When FALSE, automatic device registration will only be triggered if the MEC == 0. See SFS-2 for details.SFS-2_H-yGEDoJEeaUVpPOqbaY_w1Boolean     false
SFS2_SecurityExtensionsFRD.caldefOCSP_CHECK_FREQUENCYMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUFrequency at which OCSP requests are sent from the Back Office to the CA. _mOR4QMZQEeOtcboReKeVHA1Integer 300864001.0seconds900
SFS2_SecurityExtensionsFRD.caldefEXPIRATION_ENFORCEMENTMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUWhen performing validation, the TCS library will enforce certificate expiration if and only if the EXPIRATION_ENFORCEMENT is set to true, and only if running in ?normal? Android mode, as opposed to the recovery mode. _cbesAMZREeOtcboReKeVHA2Boolean     true
SFS5_TermsAndConditionsDemoMode.caldefPAPI_DEMO_MODE_CONFIRM_SOLD_INTERVALMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThis is the number of IGN cycles after moving from demo mode to sold mode when the Demo Mode shall be confirmed with the server on every IGN cycle. After this number of IGN cycles, the vehicle transitions into full sold mode and only checks the demo mode state every PAPI_DEMO_MODE_QUERY_CYCLE_INTERVAL IGN cycles _h5XCgLncEeS-LYW3rxjCRA2Integer 0100001.0none45
SFS5_TermsAndConditionsDemoMode.caldefTERMS_AND_CONDITIONS_BYPASS_LIMIT_COUNTMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThe limit for the Bypass Counter defined in SFS-5. If the TermsAndConditionsDialog counter exceeds this value, then Decline Terms action will be takenSFS-5, GIS-524_XYCagKyDEeSfFJuD6NTKhw3Integer 010001.0none5
SFS5_TermsAndConditionsDemoMode.caldefPAPI_DEMO_MODE_QUERY_CYCLE_INTERVALMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUSleep cycle interval between calls to pAPI to determine pAPI web demo mode status. If set to 1, a pAPI call to query demo mode status is made on each system initialization. If set to 2, a call is made every other sleep cycle and so on. _Gr3isKyEEeSfFJuD6NTKhw2Integer 110000001.0none5

SpeechRecognitionContentSettings.caldefENABLE_APPLICATIONTRAY_HANDSET_VRMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUShows Handset VR Application on application tray on startup. _yqnT6k1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Boolean     false
SpeechRecognitionContentSettings.caldefIN_PHONE_SPEECH_REC_ENABLEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUAdd new calibration IN_PHONE_SPEECH_REC_ENABLE Boolean to allow the usage of the phone?s pass-thru internal speech recognition per supplier request. $00 = Disable phone?s SR for vehicle. Phone SR stays on handset. Application and icon is removed from the Homescreen. $01 = Enable phone SR to use vehicle audio system (Speakers and microphone). Application and icon is enabled and shown on the homepage.  _yq7c_U1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Boolean     false

SpeechRecognitionPerformance.caldefEMBEDDED_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD_ARRAY_BRAZPORTUGUESEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUConfidence threshold settings for LCT (Low Confidence Threshold), HCT (High Confidence Threshold), and CSD (Confidence Score Delta) for embedded speech recognition. There shall be multiple pairs of configurable thresholds used to classify the results. Each domain based SLM shall have its own threshold pair. The combined SLM used at the highest level of the user interface shall have its own pair of thresholds. Additionally there shall be a threshold for critical commands. This critical command threshold shall be configurable for commands whose action it deemed to be difficult to reverse thus the system shall be more cautious about acting on it.GIS-381_vyyx8KHsEeaRRfXD632r1w1IntegerArray -214748364821474836471.0none[0]=4500

Each domain based SLM shall have its own threshold pair. The combined SLM used at the highest level of the user interface shall have its own pair of thresholds. SpeechRecognitionPerformance.caldefSERVER_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD_ARRAY_GERMANMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThere shall be multiple pairs of configurable thresholds used to classify the results. Each domain based SLM shall have its own threshold pair. The combined SLM used at the highest level of the user interface shall have its own pair of thresholds. Additionally there shall be a threshold for critical commands. Each domain based SLM shall have its own threshold pair. The combined SLM used at the highest level of the user interface shall have its own pair of thresholds. Each domain based SLM shall have its own threshold pair. The combined SLM used at the highest level of the user interface shall have its own pair of thresholds. Additionally there shall be a threshold for critical commands. This critical command threshold shall be configurable for commands whose action it deemed to be difficult to reverse thus the system shall be more cautious about acting on it.GIS-696_drJKwOxrEeWbjLxTTArGUQ2IntegerArray -214748364821474836471.0none[0]=500

SpeechRecognitionPerformance.caldefMAXIMUM_USER_SPEECH_TIMEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUMaximum User Speech Time - After the leading silence and before the trailing silence.GIS-696_yrOX4k1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 100300001.0none5000
SpeechRecognitionPerformance.caldefNOSPEECHTIMEOUTMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUThe period of time after the outgoing prompt during which the system is accepting audio input for recognition is called the listening window. In the event that a user doesn?t speak during the listening window the speech recognition process terminates after a configurable period of time. The system reprompts the user for the same input. No Speech TimeoutGIS-696_yrT3eE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 100100001.0none10000
SpeechRecognitionPerformance.caldefNOSPEECHTIMEOUT_EXTENDEDMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUAmount of Time that speech rec engine will wait for initial speech (i.e leading silence) on list screens with static lists (i.e non N-Best and Disambiguation)GIS-696_yrT3eU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw2Integer 100100001.0none10000

SteeringWheelSwitches_GlobalA.caldefSWC_SW14_SOURCELOW,GLOBALA,ENTRY_VCUUpper Nibble = GMLAN signal used $2 = Steering Wheel Unit #2. Lower Nibble = signal data byte $5 = Function #5. _VnRQgBlFEea1qfppV73fww1Integer 02551.0none37
TelematicsAudioControlCalibrations.caldefK_RESPONSE_TIMEOUTLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUIf, after setting AudioConnectionOutcome to REQUEST_GRANTED for a previous telematics request for non-emergency phone audio, the CSM/Low Radio does not receive AudioSourceStatus set to AUDIO_READY within K_RESPONSE_TIMEOUT then the audio management software shall cancel the internal process of the audio request. If, after setting AudioConnectionStatus to the requested stream type for a previous telematics request for non-emergency phone audio, the CSM/Low Radio does not receive AudioSourceStatus set to AUDIO_PLAYING within K_RESPONSE_TIMEOUT then the audio management software shall cancel the internal process of the audio request. GIS-616-3195_SKX_sMY9EeOsIMxD3Xbm-Q4Integer 0510020.0milliseconds150
TelematicsAudioControlCalibrations.caldefK_TELEMATICS_AUDIO_TYPELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUAn enumeration defining the medium used by the Telematics Module to stream audio data.GIS-616-3195_Crah4MY-EeOsIMxD3Xbm-Q4Enumeration     EAVB_TELEMATICS
TouchController.caldefTOUCH_INTERFACELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALBCalibration to determine interface used for touch data. This calibration is for Global A only.  _0qERMFz5EeOQzJtmpqqaoA3Enumeration     LVDSBackChannel
TouchController.caldefTOUCH_CONTROLLER_IDLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALBCalibration to determine which touch controller used in ICS. This calibration is for global A only. _4AwgMFz5EeOQzJtmpqqaoA3Enumeration     TOUCH_CONTROLLER_1
USB.caldefANALOG_INLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUEnable analog auxiliary input. See specification for further descriptionGIS-626-3820_-rugYMr3EeOpWbus8QHizA3Boolean     true
USB.caldefUSB_1_HUB_ENABLELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUUse in DTC Failure Criteria _ysLaJU1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Boolean     false
USB.caldefUSB_1_HUB_MAX_PORTSLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUUse in DTC Failure Criteria. $0 = USB1 Hub Disable. Non-zero = USB1 Hub Enable with corresponding number of maximum ports.  _ysLaJk1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 071.0none1
USB.caldefUSB_2_HUB_ENABLEMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUUse in DTC Failure Criteria _ysLaJ01AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Boolean     false
USB.caldefUSB_2_HUB_MAX_PORTSMID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,VCUUse in DTC Failure Criteria. $0 = USB2 Hub Disable. Non-zero = USB2 Hub Enable with corresponding number of maximum ports.  _ysLaKE1AEeO3UM5MzM17kw3Integer 071.0none1
USB.caldefALBUM_ART_ENABLELOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUEnable/disable display of album art in system. See Form and Behavior specification.N/A_rO8lAP0zEeOjQbz_0xXKhQ3Boolean     false
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8836REGISTERS.caldefREGISTER_0x111_BITS_7_thru_0LOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUCONTRAST CONTROL REGISTER (CONTRAST); Note as defined in the TW8836 data sheet: FUNCTION: CONTRAST; DESCRITPION: These bits control the contrast. They have value of 0 to 3.98 (FFh). A value of 100 (64h) yields a gain of 100%. The gain ranges from 0 to 255%.TECHWELL TW8836 Data Sheet_C2_PECx5EeW8XK_nvVgh2w3Integer 02551.0none92
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8836REGISTERS.caldefREGISTER_0x113_BITS_7_thru_0LOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUCHROMA (U) GAIN REGISTER (SAT_U); Note as defined in the TW8836 data sheet: FUNCTION: SAT_U ; DESCRITPION: These bits control the digital gain adjustment to the U (or Cb) component of the digital video signal. The color saturation can be adjusted by adjusting the U and V color gain components by the same amount in the normal situation. The U and V can also be adjusted independently to provide greater flexibility. The range of adjustment is 0 to 200%.TECHWELL TW8836 Data Sheet_5IKOwCx3EeW8XK_nvVgh2w3Integer 02551.0none80
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8836REGISTERS.caldefREGISTER_0x114_BITS_7_thru_0LOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUCHROMA (V) GAIN REGISTER (SAT_V); Note as defined in the TW8836 data sheet: FUNCTION: SAT_V ; DESCRIPTION: These bits control the digital gain adjustment to the V (or Cr) component of the digital video signal. The color saturation can be adjusted by adjusting the U and V color gain components by the same amount in the normal situation. The U and V can also be adjusted independently to provide greater flexibility. The range of adjustment is 0 to 200%.TECHWELL TW8836 Data Sheet_JBQq4Cx3EeW8XK_nvVgh2w3Integer 02551.0none80
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8836REGISTERS.caldefREGISTER_0x110_BITS_7_thru_0LOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUBRIGHTNESS CONTROL REGISTER (BRIGHT); Note as defined in the TW8836 data sheet: FUNCTION: BRIGHTNESS ; DESCRIPTION: These bits control the brightness. They have value of 128 to 127 in 2s complement form. Positive value increases brightness. A value 0 has no effect on the data.TECHWELL TW8836 Data Sheet_KoF7QCx2EeW8XK_nvVgh2w3Integer 02551.0none0
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8836REGISTERS.caldefREGISTER_0x115_BITS_7_thru_0LOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUHUE CONTROL REGISTER (HUE); Note as defined in the TW8836 data sheet: FUNCTION: HUE ; DESCRIPTION: These bits control the color hue. It is in 2s complement form with 0 being the center value. Positive value results in red hue and negative value gives green hue.TECHWELL TW8836 Data Sheet_mqruMCx0EeW8XK_nvVgh2w3Integer 02551.0none0
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8836REGISTERS.caldefREGISTER_0x12F_BIT2LOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUMISCELLANEOUS CONTROL III (MISC3); Note as defined in the TW8836 data sheet: FUNCTION: LCS; DESCRIPTION: 1 = Enable pre-determined output value indicated by CCS when video loss is detected, and 0 = Disable. TECHWELL TW8836 Data Sheet_vxeJACxzEeW8XK_nvVgh2w3Boolean     false
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8836REGISTERS.caldefREGISTER_0x12F_BIT3LOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUMISCELLANEOUS CONTROL III (MISC3); Note as defined in the TW8836 data sheet: FUNCTION: FCS; DESCRIPTION: 1 = Force decoder output value determined by CCS, and 0 = Disable. TECHWELL TW8836 Data Sheet_Y7b3UCxxEeW8XK_nvVgh2w3Boolean     false
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8836REGISTERS.caldefREGISTER_0x12F_BIT1LOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUMISCELLANEOUS CONTROL III (MISC3); Note as defined in the TW8836 data sheet: FUNCTION: CCS; DESCRIPTION: When FCS is set high or video loss condition is detected when LCS is set high, one of two colors display can be selected. Also 1 = Blue color, and 0 =Black. TECHWELL TW8836 Data Sheet_WPSFACxvEeW8XK_nvVgh2w3Boolean     false
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8836REGISTERS.caldefREGISTER_0x118_BITS_7_thru_6LOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUCORING CONTROL REGISTER (CORING); Note as defined in the TW8836 data sheet: FUNCTION: CTCOR ; DESCRIPTION: These bits control the coring function for the CTI. It has internal step size of 2.TECHWELL TW8836 Data Sheet_HaURUCxuEeW8XK_nvVgh2w3Integer 031.0none1
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8836REGISTERS.caldefREGISTER_0x112_BITS_5_thru_4LOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUSHARPNESS CONTROL REGISTER I (SHARPNESS); Note as defined in the TW8836 data sheet: FUNCTION: CTI ; DESCRIPTION: Color transient improvement level control. There are 4 enhancement levels with 0 being the lowest and 3 being the highest.TECHWELL TW8836 Data Sheet_uTJ1ECxsEeW8XK_nvVgh2w3Integer 031.0none1
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8836REGISTERS.caldefREGISTER_0x117_BITS_2_thru_0LOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUVERTICAL PEAKING CONTROL I ; Note as defined in the TW8836 data sheet: FUNCTION: VSHP; DESCRIPTION: Vertical peaking gain controlTECHWELL TW8836 Data Sheet_JR1T8CxrEeW8XK_nvVgh2w3Integer 071.0none0
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8836REGISTERS.caldefREGISTER_0x112_BIT7LOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUSHARPNESS CONTROL REGISTER I (SHARPNESS); Note as defined in the TW8836 data sheet: FUNCTION: SCURVE; DESCRIPTION: This bit controls the center frequency of the peaking filter. The corresponding gain adjustment is HFLT. Also, 0 = low and 1 = center.TECHWELL TW8836 Data Sheet_BHWQsCxpEeW8XK_nvVgh2w3Boolean     false
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8836REGISTERS.caldefREGISTER_0x112_BITS_3_thru_0LOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUSHARPNESS CONTROL REGISTER I (SHARPNESS); Note as defined in the TW8836 data sheet: FUNCTION: SHARP; DESCRIPTION: These bits control the amount of sharpness enhancement on the luminance signals. There are 16 levels of control with 0 having no effect on the output image and 15 being the strongest.TECHWELL TW8836 Data Sheet_3Nz1MCxnEeW8XK_nvVgh2w3Integer 0151.0none1
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8836REGISTERS.caldefREGISTER_0x07C_BIT7LOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUBT_656_ENCODE_CONTROL; Note as defined in the TW8836 data sheet: FUNCTION: ENCDITHER; DESCRIPTION: Enable BT.656 dither operation on. TECHWELL TW8836 Data Sheet_5ovdACxbEeW8XK_nvVgh2w3Boolean     false
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8836REGISTERS.caldefREGISTER_0x117_BITS_7_thru_4LOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUVERTICAL PEAKING CONTROL I ; Note as defined in the TW8836 data sheet: FUNCTION: SHCOR; DESCRIPTION: These bits provide coring function for the sharpness control.TECHWELL TW8836 Data Sheet_nThVACxiEeW8XK_nvVgh2w3Integer 0151.0none8
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8845REGISTERS_WITH_ANALOG_CAMERA.caldefTW8845_WITH_ANALOG_CAMERA_REGISTER_0x111_BITS_7_thru_0MID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALBCONTRAST CONTROL REGISTER (CONTRAST); Note as defined in the TW8845 data sheet: FUNCTION: CONTRAST; DESCRITPION: These bits control the contrast. They have value of 0 to 3.98 (FFh). A value of 100 (64h) yields a gain of 100%. The gain ranges from 0 to 255%.TECHWELL TW8845 Data Sheet_QYEUcVGIEeep_Namk9CiEQ1Integer 02551.0none100

The color saturation can be adjusted by adjusting the U and V color gain components by the same amount in the normal situation. The U and V can also be adjusted independently to provide greater flexibility. The range of adjustment is 0 to 200%.TECHWELL TW8845 Data Sheet_jEfh4VGJEeep_Namk9CiEQ1Integer 02551.0none80
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8845REGISTERS_WITH_ANALOG_CAMERA.caldefTW8845_WITH_ANALOG_CAMERA_REGISTER_0x114_BITS_7_thru_0MID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALBCHROMA (V) GAIN REGISTER (SAT_V); Note as defined in the TW8845 data sheet: FUNCTION: SAT_V ; DESCRIPTION: These bits control the digital gain adjustment to the V (or Cr) component of the digital video signal. The color saturation can be adjusted by adjusting the U and V color gain components by the same amount in the normal situation. The U and V can also be adjusted independently to provide greater flexibility. The range of adjustment is 0 to 200%.TECHWELL TW8845 Data Sheet_aRp90VGKEeep_Namk9CiEQ1Integer 02551.0none80
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8845REGISTERS_WITH_ANALOG_CAMERA.caldefTW8845_WITH_ANALOG_CAMERA_REGISTER_0x110_BITS_7_thru_0MID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALBBRIGHTNESS CONTROL REGISTER (BRIGHT); Note as defined in the TW8845 data sheet: FUNCTION: BRIGHTNESS ; DESCRIPTION: These bits control the contrast. They have a value of 0 to 3.98 (FFh). A value of 100.TECHWELL TW8845 Data Sheet_IZfM8VGLEeep_Namk9CiEQ1Integer 02551.0none0
VIDEO_DECODER_TECHWELL_TW8845REGISTERS_WITH_ANALOG_CAMERA.caldefTW8845_WITH_ANALOG_CAMERA_REGISTER_0x115_BITS_7_thru_0MID,HIGH,GLOBALA,GLOBALBHUE CONTROL REGISTER (HUE); Note as defined in the TW8845 data sheet: FUNCTION: HUE ; DESCRIPTION: These bits control the color hue. VehicleSpecificEqualization.caldefVSE_PARAM_EQ_FUNCTION_BAND_5_LFLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUThe functional calibrations referred to above shall also allow the remaining bands not used by the PEQ to be defined as additional Phase Shifts, Shelving Treble Filters, or Shelving Bass filters. While being used for this function, certain PEQ tuning calibrations will not apply to the current function, this is defined in Table 20 following this section. GIS-604-430_kcp8MdF2EeSWPPCziDJYrA3Enumeration     PEQ

VehicleSpecificEqualization.caldefVSE_PARAM_EQ_R_HRLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUThe CSM and Low radio shall provide two additional boost calibrations to allow the development engineer to set the final headroom for the Front and Rear channels once values for all parametric EQ have been finalized. This setting ensures that the system can reach its maximum loudness specification. There is one setting for the Front channels and a second for the Rear.GIS-604-440_VPDjkcY7EeOsIMxD3Xbm-Q3Float 0.024.00.1dB0.0
VehicleSpecificEqualization.caldefVSE_PARAM_EQ_F_HRLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCUThe CSM and Low radio shall provide two additional boost calibrations to allow the development engineer to set the final headroom for the Front and Rear channels once values for all parametric EQ have been finalized. This setting ensures that the system can reach its maximum loudness specification. There is one setting for the Front channels and a second for the Rear.GIS-604-440_H-1DoMY7EeOsIMxD3Xbm-Q3Float 0.024.00.1dB0.0

VehicleSpecificEqualization.caldefDelay_Length1_InLOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,MID,HIGHDelay line of front leftLGE Arkamys Cal_LBXc0cbHEeWf-sP7g1vSuw3Integer -214748364821474836471.0none1
VolumeGroupRampTimes.caldefMUTE_RAMP_VOL_GROUP_VOICE_CALLLOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,MID,HIGH,VCUVolumeGroups organize similar audio streams together under a common volume index. Since users may move through the various VolumeGroups supporting the Mutex Sink, with the possibility of each volume group being at a different step of the index, it is import to ensure that these transitions produce a pleasing audio experience. To facilitate this, the calibrateable ramp times in GIS-604-650 are used to define the mute/unmute ramp rates used when an audio stream is activated or deactivated according to their VolumeGroup.GIS-604-825 _w7t1AWX2EeSy4KUHhd_sAg3Enumeration     K_MUTEX_SINK_RAMP_1
VolumeGroupRampTimes.caldefMUTE_RAMP_VOL_GROUP_RINGLOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,MID,HIGH,VCUVolumeGroups organize similar audio streams together under a common volume index. Since users may move through the various VolumeGroups supporting the Mutex Sink, with the possibility of each volume group being at a different step of the index, it is import to ensure that these transitions produce a pleasing audio experience. To facilitate this, the calibrateable ramp times in GIS-604-650 are used to define the mute/unmute ramp rates used when an audio stream is activated or deactivated according to their VolumeGroup.GIS-604-825 _wQBMoWX2EeSy4KUHhd_sAg3Enumeration     K_MUTEX_SINK_RAMP_2

Since users may move through the various VolumeGroups supporting the Mutex Sink, with the possibility of each volume group being at a different step of the index, it is import to ensure that these transitions produce a pleasing audio experience. To facilitate this, the calibrateable ramp times in GIS-604-650 are used to define the mute/unmute ramp rates used when an audio stream is activated or deactivated according to their VolumeGroup.GIS-604-825 _wbD_AGX2EeSy4KUHhd_sAg3Enumeration     K_MUTEX_SINK_RAMP_3

VolumeGroupRampTimes.caldefMUTE_RAMP_VOL_GROUP_AUDIO_CUELOW,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,MID,HIGH,VCUVolumeGroups organize similar audio streams together under a common volume index. Since users may move through the various VolumeGroups supporting the Mutex Sink, with the possibility of each volume group being at a different step of the index, it is import to ensure that these transitions produce a pleasing audio experience. To facilitate this, the calibrateable ramp times in GIS-604-650 are used to define the mute/unmute ramp rates used when an audio stream is activated or deactivated according to their VolumeGroup.GIS-604-825 _v9w_AWX2EeSy4KUHhd_sAg3Enumeration     K_MUTEX_SINK_RAMP_2

WiFiInfo3.caldefMaxTransmitPowerLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUMaximum Transmit Power at the WiFi AntennaGIS331-1311 Transmit Power_TqEqQMSwEeOL876Etsd7Eg3Integer 0301.0none0
WiFiInfo3.caldefWiFiP2PDeviceNameLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUWiFiP2PDeviceName is the name of the Wi-Fi Module in the P2P mode. It shall be the same for the P2P Group Owner and Group Client roles. WiFiP2PDeviceName depends on the vehicle model and brand. The WiFiP2PDeviceName shall be the same as the Bluetooth_Module_Device_Name. GIS 331-3240_dQX7gMTDEeOzhbtZfiZNyg3String  32 UTF8
WiFiInfo3.caldefWiFiClientDeviceNameLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUName of the Wi-Fi Module in the Wi-Fi Client Mode (STA mode). The WiFi Client Device Name shall be dependent on the vehicle model and brand. The WiFiClientDeviceName shall be the same as the Bluetooth_Module_Device_Name. GIS 331-3240_wbtpoMSwEeOL876Etsd7Eg3String  32 UTF8
WiFiInfo3.caldefWiFiNumSupportedDevicesLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCU WiFiNumSupportedDevices is the maximum number of Wi-Fi devices that can be supported by the Wi-Fi Module (for consumer devices in P2P mode and STA mode)GIS-331 4111,5214_CBVcgMSxEeOL876Etsd7Eg3Integer 0201.0none0
WiFiInfo3.caldefNumWiFiDevicesFilteredLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUNumWiFiDevicesFiltered describes the maximum number of consumer devices (in STA mode) that can be allowed to connect to the WiFi Module _67Zr4cTBEeOzhbtZfiZNyg3Integer 0201.0none0
WiFiInfo3.caldefNumWiFiP2PDevicesFilteredLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALB,ENTRY_VCU,VCUNumWiFiP2PDevicesFiltered describes the maximum number of devices (in P2P mode) that can be allowed to connect to the WiFi Module _1p7lgMSxEeOL876Etsd7Eg2Integer 0201.0none0
WiFiInfo3.caldefEnableVTMLOW,MID,HIGH,GEM,GLOBALA,GLOBALBEnableVTM is for enabling the VTM triggers for the VTM over WiFi data. GIS 331_UfzPAMTJEeOzhbtZfiZNyg3Boolean     false
